meta.description: Unified causal graph encoding financial market mental models as a dense network of concepts, edges, feedback loops, chains, regimes, axioms, and forks.
meta.id_naming_philosophy: Concept IDs serve as query seeds for an LLM agent that receives NO definitions or descriptions -- only the bare ID strings. Every ID must therefore be immediately recognizable as a standard finance/markets/economics term. Avoid: proprietary metaphors (e.g. 'ouroboros', 'dragon_portfolio'), editorial opinions disguised as concepts (e.g. 'survey_data_broken'), opaque acronyms without context (e.g. 'BNOC', 'MAED'), nature/military/mythology metaphors, and overly casual names. Prefer: clear descriptive finance terminology that an analyst or LLM would naturally use when searching (e.g. 'fiscal_dominance', 'term_premium', 'option_adjusted_basis'). All concepts must be timeless and universal -- no time-bound references (e.g. 'post_covid'), no country-specific narratives unless the concept itself is a durable structural pattern, and no esoteric terms-of-art invented by a single author that lack broader recognition.

consolidation_guidance.description: Framework for deciding whether two candidate concepts should remain separate nodes or be merged. Applied during the v1.0 consolidation that reduced the graph from 223 to ~146 concepts.
merge_when: One concept is a threshold or phase of the other (e.g. debt_saturation is just a level of debt_to_gdp)
One concept is a measurement caveat or data limitation about the other (e.g. CPI limitations qualifies inflation)
One concept is a sub-mechanism or specific implementation detail within the other (e.g. EOM option is one delivery option component)
The child concept never appears as an independent causal driver -- it only modifies or qualifies the parent
Removing the child node and enriching the parent description preserves every causal pathway in the graph
The child has no incoming edges from concepts outside the parent's immediate domain cluster
The child and parent always co-move -- you would never observe one changing direction without the other
keep_separate_when: Each concept has independent incoming AND outgoing edges to different domains (cross-domain bridging power)
They can move in opposite directions or decouple (e.g. realized vol can fall while implied vol rises)
They operate on genuinely different timescales (e.g. credit_impulse is quarterly, debt_to_gdp is secular)
They have distinct attribution sources reflecting genuinely different mental models, not just different chapters of the same source
Merging would require if-then branching in the description -- a sign of two distinct causal nodes wearing one label
They appear on opposite sides of a fork, gating variable, or regime switch
Each independently gates or constrains a third concept with different conditions
consolidation_guidance.litmus_test: Delete concept X and route all its edges through concept Y. Does any causal chain in the graph lose explanatory power, become ambiguous, or require a conditional clause that was not there before? If no: merge. If yes: keep separate.

concepts.label: Credit Impulse (Delta-Delta Credit)
concepts.description: Second derivative of total debt creation (public + private). Drives nominal demand, profits, and asset prices. Impulse matters, not level.

concepts.label: Private Credit Growth
concepts.description: Bank lending expansion. ~3x GDP multiplier vs public deficit. Demand-driven, constrained by capital ratios not reserves.

concepts.label: Government Deficit Spending
concepts.description: Fiscal deficit creates private savings via sectoral balance identity. The engine of nominal growth when private credit is flat.

concepts.label: Nominal Demand
concepts.description: Total nominal spending in the economy. Downstream of credit impulse with ~4-6 quarter lag.

concepts.label: Corporate Profits
concepts.description: Proportional to total debt (Kalecki: Pi ~ 3.5% x Total Debt). A balance-sheet variable, not a productivity variable.

concepts.label: Asset Prices
concepts.description: Equity and real estate valuations. Driven by credit impulse, wealth effect, and risk premiums.

concepts.label: Collateral Values
concepts.description: Value of assets pledged against loans. Rising collateral enables more lending (reflexive loop).

concepts.label: Debt/GDP Ratio (Omega)
concepts.description: Stock of debt relative to income. AR(1) persistence of 0.96. The missing state variable in macro.

concepts.label: Endogenous Money Creation
concepts.description: Loans create deposits simultaneously. Banks do not lend reserves. Credit is the active monetary variable; M2 is a residual.

concepts.label: Bank Lending Standards (SLOOS)
concepts.description: Senior Loan Officer Survey. Leads credit growth by ~12 months. Tightening precedes slowdown.

concepts.label: Debt Service Ratio
concepts.description: Interest payments as share of income. Must stay in tolerable band (~10-15%). Forces yields down when Omega rises.

concepts.label: Treasury General Account Balance
concepts.description: Treasury's checking account at the Fed. Dynamic risk policy floor based on 1-week forward outflows. Thermostat mechanism.

concepts.label: Bills vs Coupons Gate
concepts.description: Whether deficit is funded via bills (stimulative, cash-equivalent) or coupons (restrictive, duration risk). The most impactful fiscal decision.

concepts.label: Extraordinary Measures (Debt Ceiling)
concepts.description: G-Fund suspension (~$300B capacity). Depletes daily until X-date. Buffer-until-break pattern.

concepts.label: Reverse Repo (RRP) Balance
concepts.description: MMF cash parked at Fed overnight. Drains when bill yields exceed RRP rate. Structural floor exists.

concepts.label: Bank Reserves
concepts.description: Aggregate reserves at Fed. Distribution matters more than level -- pockets of scarcity cause stress.

concepts.label: Money Market Fund Allocation
concepts.description: MMFs choose between bills, RRP, private repo, FHLB floaters based on yield spread and WAM constraints.

concepts.label: Repo Market Rates
concepts.description: Collateralized lending rates. GC tracks Fed Funds. Specials reflect specific security demand. Financing cost for all positions.

concepts.label: Federal Funds Rate
concepts.description: Overnight interbank rate. Set within Fed corridor. FHLB-FBO-IORB arbitrage chain keeps it in range.

concepts.label: Interest on Reserve Balances
concepts.description: Rate Fed pays on reserves. Ceiling-adjacent rate. When Fed pays more than portfolio yield, small-scale money printing.

concepts.label: Dealer Balance Sheet Capacity
concepts.description: SLR and leverage ratio constrain dealer intermediation. The binding constraint on market liquidity. Nettable vs unnettable repos matter.

concepts.label: Fed Rate Corridor (Floor-Ceiling)
concepts.description: RRP floor, IORB near-ceiling, SRF/DW ceiling. Self-correcting: rates above ceiling trigger facility usage, below floor trigger RRP absorption.

concepts.label: Settlement / Fedwire Mechanics
concepts.description: DVP closes 3:30 PM, triparty 3:30-6:00 PM. Timing mismatches create daylight overdrafts. BNY is sole triparty agent.

concepts.label: Quarter-End Window Dressing
concepts.description: Banks shrink balance sheets for reporting. Shift from unnettable to nettable repos. Predictable rate spikes at quarter/year-end.

concepts.label: FHLB Hidden Connector
concepts.description: FHLBs dont earn IORB, so lend via fed funds to foreign banks who arbitrage. Primary mechanism keeping EFFR in target range.

concepts.label: Standing Repo Facility (SRF)
concepts.description: Rate ceiling enforcement. Stigma limits usage. TOMOs (no stigma) used as alternative. Rate cap mechanism.

concepts.label: FX Swap / Dollar Funding
concepts.description: Dollar funding for foreign banks via basis swaps and central bank swap lines. Cross-border plumbing stress channel.

concepts.label: Hedge Fund Leverage via Repo
concepts.description: Cash to repo to treasury to repo to treasury. Leverage stacking via sponsored repo. Basis trade growth.

concepts.label: Tax Withholding Receipts
concepts.description: Real-time labor market signal. Daily DTS data faster than BLS. Core 20 business day baseline with seasonal overlays.

concepts.label: Seasonal Treasury Cash Flows
concepts.description: April 15 tax surge, SS payment schedule, GSE remittance cycle, quarter-end flows. Predictable periodic patterns.

concepts.label: Term Premium
concepts.description: Compensation for bearing duration risk beyond rate expectations. Supply-demand determined. The actual stance of financial conditions.

concepts.label: Financial Conditions
concepts.description: Composite of rates, spreads, equity prices, dollar. Term premium, not fed funds, determines actual tightness.

concepts.label: Wealth Effect
concepts.description: Top 10% of households drive ~50% of consumption. Equity valuations transmit financial conditions to real economy.

concepts.label: Equity Multiples (P/E)
concepts.description: Inversely related to long real yields. Certainty premiums (government backstops) justify structurally higher multiples.

concepts.label: Long-End Yields (10Y+)
concepts.description: Composite: expectations + term premium + convexity bias. The bond market is everything -- all assets price off the risk-free long rate.

concepts.label: Short-End Rates
concepts.description: Governed by CB policy path. 2Y yield = 24-month rolling window of monetary policy expectations.

concepts.label: Inflation
concepts.description: Lagged accounting cascade: Credit -> Assets -> Prices -> Wages. House prices lead shelter CPI by 16 months.

concepts.label: Inflation Expectations
concepts.description: 5Y5Y forward reacts to, never propels, the cycle. Wages follow prices adaptively (1Q lag), not expectations.

concepts.label: Fed Reaction Function
concepts.description: Dual mandate pendulum between inflation and employment. Committee divergence brakes speed of repricing. Political actor constrained by incentives.

concepts.label: Fiscal Dominance
concepts.description: When debt/GDP is high, fiscal flows dominate monetary policy. Deficit is the engine, rates are the steering wheel.

concepts.label: Policy Backstop Bias
concepts.description: Policymakers always intervene to prevent disorderly bond market. Balancing loop that fights the correction the economy needs.

concepts.label: Spot Money vs Future Money
concepts.description: Protecting bonds (future money) devalues currency (spot money). Cannot save both simultaneously.

concepts.label: Savings-Investment Balance
concepts.description: Determines sensitivity of long yields to public debt. AI capex and Chinas surplus are the two largest structural forces.

concepts.label: Rate Outcome Distribution
concepts.description: Right tail (higher yields) vs left tail (lower yields). Distribution shape matters more than point forecast. Inflation is the gatekeeper.

concepts.label: Tariff Transmission
concepts.description: Either price pass-through (inflationary but reshores) or foreign margin absorption (non-inflationary but doesnt reshore). Successful tariffs require some inflation.

concepts.label: Uncertainty as Economic Damage
concepts.description: Uncertainty itself freezes hiring and capex even when policy doesnt fully materialize. No hire, no fire stall speed.

concepts.label: Fed-Treasury Coordination
concepts.description: Bill-heavy issuance + Fed RMPs = implicit monetization. Stealth Operation Twist. Both institutions want lower long-end yields.

concepts.label: AI Disinflation Paradox
concepts.description: AI is structurally disinflationary (productivity) but short-run reflationary (wealth effect from AI equity valuations). Paradox of coexisting forces.

concepts.label: Implied Volatility
concepts.description: Market price of expected future volatility. Systematically exceeds realized vol (vol risk premium). Encodes distribution beliefs.

concepts.label: Realized Volatility
concepts.description: Actual observed price variation. Hedging frequency determines which realized vol you sample. Zero-mean vs standard matters.

concepts.label: Gamma Exposure (GEX)
concepts.description: Net gamma of dealer hedging book. Positive GEX stabilizes (dealers buy dips, sell rips). Near zero = neutral. Negative = destabilizing.

concepts.label: Vanna Exposure (VEX)
concepts.description: Dealer vanna exposure. When puts go ITM, rising IV forces dealers to sell underlying. Self-reinforcing crash mechanism.

concepts.label: Gamma-Theta Contest
concepts.description: Daily PnL = 0.5*Gamma*(Move)^2 - Theta*dt. If realized > implied, long gamma wins. Fundamental options trade-off.

concepts.label: Volatility Surface
concepts.description: Strike x expiry grid of implied vols. Skew = spot-vol correlation belief. Term structure = mean-reversion belief. Sticky strike vs floating regimes.

concepts.label: Volatility Risk Premium (VRP)
concepts.description: IV systematically exceeds RV. Sellers earn premium for bearing tail risk. Not free money -- compensates for catastrophic loss potential.

concepts.label: Dispersion / Correlation
concepts.description: Index vol = avg stock vol x avg correlation. Crashes: both vol AND correlation spike (multiplicative). Basket of options > option on basket.

concepts.label: Put-Call Parity Identity
concepts.description: C = (S-K) + P + cost_of_carry. Covered call = naked put. Connects every concept in options. Deepest single relationship.

concepts.label: Dealer Positioning
concepts.description: Dealer delta-hedging obligations form hidden liquidity layer. The implied order book dominates visible limit order book.

concepts.label: Consensus / Crowded Positioning
concepts.description: When positioning reaches extremes, highest-probability move inflicts maximum pain. Fragile equilibria resolve violently.

concepts.label: Market Narrative Lifecycle
concepts.description: Narratives persist -> consensus aligns -> confirming data arrives -> if price doesnt respond = exhaustion signal -> reversal.

concepts.label: VAR Shock / Crowded Unwind
concepts.description: Volatility events target most crowded positions. Uniform positioning -> fragile equilibrium -> any catalyst -> liquidation cascade.

concepts.label: Correlation Break as Regime Signal
concepts.description: When a correlation that should hold doesnt (e.g. USD not rallying on tariffs), something structural has changed. Earliest observable regime-change tell.

concepts.label: Price Over Narrative
concepts.description: When price and narrative diverge, trust price. What matters is not WHY but WHAT the market is doing.

concepts.label: Event Volatility Premium
concepts.description: Markets systematically overprice known-event vol. Realized move rarely exceeds implied breakeven. Fade front-end.

concepts.label: Current Account Balance
concepts.description: CA deficit = capital inflow (identity). US as 60% of global deficit = structural demand for US assets from surplus countries.

concepts.label: Cross-Border Capital Flows
concepts.description: Post-GFC shift: bank paper -> equities. Composition determines which US assets benefit. Foreign CBs diversifying away from USTs.

subsumes.label: Portfolio Capital Flow Dominance in FX

concepts.label: Commodity Prices
concepts.description: Downstream of credit cycle via stocks -> dollar -> oil chain. Causality runs from commodities to dollar, not reverse (consensus has it backward).

concepts.label: US Dollar
concepts.description: Master cross-asset regime variable. Dollar/yield decoupling signals regime change. Currencies are shares of global production (GDP-FX parity).

concepts.label: Surplus Country Trap
concepts.description: High debt + CA surplus = trapped. Must maintain surplus or face sudden stop. Japan and China cannot easily pivot. NIIP buffer is finite.

concepts.label: BOP / Sectoral Balance Identity
concepts.description: Private balance + government balance + foreign balance = 0. One sectors deficit is mechanically anothers surplus.

concepts.label: Commodity-Dollar Cycle (10yr)
concepts.description: Commodities up -> EM earnings -> capital to EM -> USD down -> commodities up (reinforcing). Reversal: vicious cycle in other direction.

concepts.label: US-China Economic Interdependence
concepts.description: US (deficit/consumer) and China (surplus/producer) locked in interdependence. Debt = fissile material, tariffs = nuclear button.

concepts.label: Flight-to-Quality Status
concepts.description: Whether Treasuries retain safe-haven status. Tested at each risk-off episode. Negative equity-Treasury correlation = intact. Positive = impaired.

concepts.label: Exchange Rates
concepts.description: Piecewise cointegrated with relative real GDP. GDP-FX parity: currencies price relative economic equity. Credit drives FX through GDP and assets.

concepts.label: Crisis vs Panic Discriminator
concepts.description: Panics: positioning flush + no credit deterioration = buy the dip. Crises: credit supply freeze + secured lending losses = defend. Most drawdowns are panics.

concepts.label: Credit Supply Freeze
concepts.description: The crisis signature. Not demand decline but supply freeze. Unexpected losses on safe secured lending spike provisions 50-100x.

concepts.label: Provisioning Spike in Secured Lending
concepts.description: Losses in secured (not unsecured) lending cause system shock. Each crisis originates in a NEW safe asset class.

concepts.label: Plumbing / Funding Stress
concepts.description: Reserve drain hits pockets of scarcity. Repo rate spikes, forced selling, independent of economic deterioration. Sept 2019 template.

concepts.label: Minsky Financial Instability
concepts.description: Hedge -> speculative -> Ponzi finance. Stability breeds instability. Period of calm builds leverage that makes eventual correction violent.

concepts.label: Loss Buffer Exhaustion
concepts.description: Stabilizer has finite capacity, then becomes accelerant. Partial claims suppress DLQ until exhausted. Emergency tools suppress vol until crowding.

concepts.label: Bond Price-Yield Convexity
concepts.description: Price gains from yield drops exceed losses from equal yield rises. Convexity is valuable. Second-order effect dominates in large moves.

concepts.label: Carry and Rolldown
concepts.description: Holding return = coupon carry + rolldown as curve ages position. Carry-to-volatility ratio is the proper risk-adjusted measure.

concepts.label: Duration as Sensitivity
concepts.description: Modified duration translates yield changes to price changes. DV01 normalizes across instruments. Yield betas adjust for cross-sector hedging.

concepts.label: Swap Spread Decomposition
concepts.description: Swap rate minus Treasury yield = credit + funding + supply/demand. Mortgage hedging is the most powerful short-term driver.

concepts.label: Mortgage Negative Convexity Feedback
concepts.description: Rates rise -> prepayments fall -> MBS duration extends -> hedgers sell -> rates rise more. Self-reinforcing spiral. The dominant flow in rates.

concepts.label: Treasury Futures Basis / CTD
concepts.description: Basis = cash - futures*CF. Net basis = delivery option value. CTD switches at 6% pivot. Futures systematically cheap by option premium.

concepts.label: Supply/Demand for Duration
concepts.description: Foreign CBs buy intermediate. Pensions buy long-end. Banks buy front. Hedge funds lever via basis trade. Structural shifts move specific curve sectors.

concepts.label: Yield Curve Shape
concepts.description: PCA: level (~85%), slope (~10%), curvature (~3%). Bull steep = Fed cuts. Bear flat = Fed hikes. Curve is directional trade when terminal anchored.

concepts.label: Rate Options / Swaptions
concepts.description: Add volatility dimension to rates. Gamma/theta fundamental trade-off. Conditional trades isolate desired exposure. Vol surface in rates encodes dominant risk.

concepts.label: On-the-Run / Off-the-Run Spread
concepts.description: Most recently issued = most liquid, often on special in repo. Older issues trade cheap. Spread = liquidity premium.

concepts.label: Treasury Auction Demand
concepts.description: Stop-through/tail, dealer allocation, end-user participation. Auctions bring out buyers. Higher yields -> stronger sponsorship -> self-correcting.

concepts.label: Yield Range Consolidation
concepts.description: 10Y oscillates within range until catalyst breaks out. Bollinger band compression signals imminent move. New range forms at shifted level.

concepts.label: Implied Equity Risk Premium
concepts.description: Solved backward from market prices, cash flows, growth expectations. Best single gauge of market valuation. Far superior to PE ratios.

concepts.label: DCF / Intrinsic Value
concepts.description: PV of expected cash flows. Four inputs: revenue growth, margins, reinvestment, risk. Earnings are not cash flows.

concepts.label: Competitive Advantage Decay
concepts.description: Excess returns exist only with moats. Width (ROIC-WACC) and depth (duration). Competition relentlessly erodes. 70%+ of firms earn below cost of capital.

concepts.label: Aggregate Revenue Overclaim
concepts.description: Big new market -> overconfident entrepreneurs + VCs -> individually plausible, collectively fairy tale. Sum of break-even revenues > TAM.

concepts.label: Equity as Call Option on Assets
concepts.description: Equity = max(V-D, 0). Time and volatility keep equity alive when intrinsic = 0. Distressed equity pricing, wealth transfer to equity from debt.

concepts.label: Gold Catastrophe Hedge
concepts.description: Collectible, not asset. Cannot be valued, only priced. Weak hedge against normal inflation. Strong hedge against hyperinflation/catastrophe.

concepts.label: Stock-Bond Correlation Regime
concepts.description: Depends on CB attitude toward inflation tail, not rate levels. Negative correlation (60/40 works) only when CB unconstrained. Not physics, just a regime.

concepts.label: Vol Reflexivity Loop
concepts.description: ~$2T of financial engineering strategies simultaneously drive and are driven by vol. Lower vol feeds lower vol until exogenous shock reverses violently.

concepts.label: Short Volatility Crowding
concepts.description: Modern institutional portfolio is ~98% short vol and doesnt know it. Covered calls, risk parity, vol targeting -- all secretly the same bet.

concepts.label: Regime Diversification
concepts.description: Diversify across macro regimes (growth, deflation, inflation, squeeze), not across asset classes that are secretly the same short-vol bet.

concepts.label: Suppressed Risk Reemergence
concepts.description: CB suppression transmutes risk from price mechanism to hidden tail risk and social instability. The longer suppression, the larger eventual realization.

concepts.label: CapEx Inherent Cyclicality
concepts.description: Every this-time-is-different capex boom ends in a glut. AI capex follows the same arc. Picks and shovels work until they dont.

concepts.label: Cross-Market Rate Spillovers
concepts.description: Japan is primary exporter of bearish long-end shocks. JGB moves transmit 2-3bp per 10bp shock to US/DE/UK. Global long-end views must incorporate Japan.

concepts.label: Quarterly SPX Collar Roll
concepts.description: JPM hedged equity fund rolls a massive SPX collar quarterly. Creates periodic inelastic buy/sell flow at predictable strikes. Local turning points around roll dates.

concepts.label: Foreign Capital Diversification / Exit
concepts.description: Sanctions, protectionism, or reserve diversification trigger foreign selling of USTs. Yields up + USD down simultaneously. Structural shift in who finances the deficit.

concepts.label: Passive Investing Flow Amplification
concepts.description: Passive dominance removes volatility buffers. Each incremental buyer/seller amplifies tail risk. Markets become entirely flow-driven. The drunk friend wanders off the cliff.

concepts.label: Credit-Volatility Linkage
concepts.description: Credit regime shifts drive volatility regime shifts. Vol is the brother of credit. To forecast vol, track credit conditions and solvency, not vol itself.

concepts.label: Centrally Cleared Treasury Market
concepts.description: FICC netting provides balance sheet relief for dealers. Shifts who can intermediate. Sponsored repo via FICC allows hedge funds direct access. Changes the liquidity topology.

concepts.label: Systemic Risk Conditions
concepts.description: Monitor conditions (leverage, short-vol crowding, liquidity, valuations), not sparks (catalysts). Conditions determine if any spark becomes conflagration. Pattern match across 1928/1987/1998/2008.

concepts.label: Vol Persistence Regime (Low vs High)
concepts.description: Low persistence (modern): vol spikes -> CB rescues -> mean reverts. Short vol works. High persistence (1930s/1970s): vol spikes -> CB constrained -> stays elevated for YEARS. Long vol becomes positive carry. Inflation is the regime switch.

concepts.label: Regime-Dependent Contrarianism
concepts.description: Extreme positioning is contrarian in mean-reverting assets (GBP, CAD) but continuation in trending assets (USDJPY, equities in bull). Must classify asset as mean-reverting or trending BEFORE applying sentiment signal.

concepts.label: Crypto Risk Appetite Signal
concepts.description: Bitcoin/crypto leads risk appetite. No fundamental valuation constraint -> pure positioning. Crypto turns lower while QQQ chugs -> equities follow with lag. Shitcoin rallies = peak retail FOMO.

concepts.label: CB Policy Divergence Engine (G4+)
concepts.description: Structural growth/inflation mix differences across Fed/ECB/BOE/BOJ/BOC create exploitable cross-market rate spreads. Front-end divergence persists (domestic CB path); long-end divergence bounded (global risk premium correlated).

concepts.label: Curve Segmentation Machine (Front/Belly/Long)
concepts.description: Three distinct machines: front-end (CB policy path), belly (terminal rate + convexity), long-end (risk premium + supply + cross-border). A single macro factor rarely moves the whole curve. Curve trades are decomposition bets.

concepts.label: Variable-Rate Economy Vulnerability
concepts.description: US fixed-rate mortgages vs variable-rate economies (UK, AUS, Canada, Nordics). Rate sensitivity diverges massively. CB divergence trade: weak-link economies crack first, transmit back to US via FX and trade.

concepts.label: Carry-to-Volatility Ratio
concepts.description: = (carry + rolldown, annualized) / (duration x yield vol, annualized). THE comparison metric across instruments, tenors, and trade structures. Higher ratio = carry earned more efficiently relative to MTM uncertainty. >0.5 attractive, <0.2 poor.

concepts.label: Carry Trade Crowding Detection
concepts.description: Three signals: (1) asymmetric price response -- muted on favorable, outsized on unfavorable, (2) cross-market carry correlation spike -- unrelated carry trades become correlated, (3) position data buildup toward historical extremes. Months of carry erased in days.

concepts.label: Dislocation Z-Score Framework
concepts.description: Z = (current level - long-run average) / vol of spread. Standardizes RV attractiveness: 30bp dislocation in 5bp-vol spread = compelling; 30bp in 100bp-vol spread = noise. Compare each bonds current residual to its OWN history, not to zero.

concepts.label: Swap Spread Driver Rotation
concepts.description: Same spread, completely different dominant factor depending on regime. Bank credit (crisis), mortgage hedging (day-to-day), duration extension (after sustained low rates), Treasury supply (structural), pension demand (long-end), corporate issuance (seasonal).

concepts.label: Cheapest-to-Deliver Selection
concepts.description: Two orthogonal rules operating simultaneously. Duration rule: yields > ref -> high-duration CTD; yields < ref -> low-duration CTD. Yield rule: among similar-duration bonds, highest-yielding is CTD. Spread changes can override duration rule.

concepts.label: Squeeze Mechanics (Buffer Until Break)
concepts.description: Deliverable supply absorbs OI until physical constraint binds. Pre-break: normal options dynamics. Post-break: failure-probability regime. Bond goes special -> OI exceeds float -> RP spirals to 0 -> BNOC negative. Paradox: most attractive short basis = greatest squeeze risk.

concepts.label: Option-Adjusted Basis
concepts.description: OAB = observed BNOC - theoretical delivery option value. OAB > 0: basis rich, futures cheap, sell basis. OAB < 0: basis cheap, futures rich, buy basis. Cross-checks basis model against listed options via ATM bond futures call comparison.

concepts.label: Yield Beta Regime Break
concepts.description: 5Y swap vs 5Y Tsy: R²=0.99 in normal, R²=0.18 in crisis. Betas estimated in calm may be wrong in stress. Yield beta 0.8 means raw DV01 ratio overhedges by 25%. Treasury hedges fail in flight-to-quality; swap hedges track credit better.

concepts.label: Structural Vol Supply/Demand Flows
concepts.description: Demand: GSE portfolios buy long-expiry intermediate-tail (3Yx10Y), mortgage servicers buy short-expiry. Supply: callable issuers sell long-end swaptions, range accrual issuance depresses cap vol. Shapes the vol surface independent of rate expectations.

concepts.label: Shadow Money / Collateral Velocity
concepts.description: Shadow money = outstanding x price x (1-haircut). Collateral velocity (rehypothecation churn) multiplies effective stock. Three multiplicative factors: small moves compound. Velocity fell 3.0 to 2.4 (2007-2010) = 20% invisible monetary contraction.

concepts.label: Haircut Procyclicality
concepts.description: Haircuts compress in booms (easy terms, confidence high), spike in crises (tight terms, confidence evaporating). Haircut cycle LEADS credit cycle. More sensitive leading indicator than interest rates. Parallels Minsky progression.

concepts.label: Specials Lending Income
concepts.description: Owning a bond on special = earning lending fee = (GC rate - specials rate) x notional x (days/360). Independent of bond price P/L. OTR specialness is predictable (auction cycle) and persistent. Separate revenue stream from carry.

concepts.label: On-the-Run Bond Lifecycle
concepts.description: OTR (most liquid, special, rich, tightest bid-ask) -> first off-the-run (liquidity drops, specialness fades) -> double/triple-old (progressively less liquid, can become CTD) -> deep off-the-run (minimal liquidity, held by buy-and-hold). Richness decays predictably.

concepts.label: Futures Negative Convexity
concepts.description: Unlike regular bonds, futures have OPPOSITE convexity. Yields rise -> CTD shifts to higher duration -> amplifies loss. Yields fall -> CTD shifts to lower duration -> curtails gain. Futures hedge leaves you short convexity. In volatile markets, futures underperform cash.

concepts.label: LCR / HQLA Stack / Intraday Liquidity
concepts.description: LCR = HQLA / Net 30-day stressed outflows >= 100%. L1 (100%): reserves, Tsys. L2A (85%): agency MBS. Intraday RLAP/RLEN often MORE binding than end-of-day LCR. FHLB advances boost LCR: proceeds = L1 HQLA, FHLB = low outflow rate. Only FHLBs provide net-positive boost.

concepts.label: Foreign Bank Reserve Deployment
concepts.description: FBOs borrow FF from FHLBs at IORB-8bp. Park: deposit at Fed earning IORB (risk-free 8bp spread). Deploy: into GCF repos (+15bp), DVP repos (+20bp), or FX swaps (+50bp in stress). Park-vs-deploy decision determines exactly where EFFR settles daily.

concepts.label: Front-End Rate Hierarchy / Spread Signals
concepts.description: Stable hierarchy: specials < bills < triparty < GCF < SOFR < EFFR < IORB < SRF. Spread relationships encode counterparty risk, collateral scarcity, access constraints, and settlement pressure. Hierarchy inversion = crisis signal.

concepts.label: Fed Rate Targeting Zone
concepts.description: Fed targets SOFR at IORB-1 to 5bp. Headwinds: quarter-end, tax settlement, HF leverage demand, bank unrealized losses. Tailwinds: RRP absorption, T-bill supply, central clearing netting. Tools: adjust administered rates, ad hoc repos, QT pace.

concepts.label: Broad Money + Shadow Money
concepts.description: Traditional M2 is incomplete. Shadow money = sum(outstanding x price x (1-haircut)) across all collateral. Effective money = M2 + shadow money. Three multiplicative factors (value, haircut, velocity) make shadow money the invisible monetary variable. 2008: 48% shadow money destruction.

concepts.label: QT Reserve Drain Cliff
concepts.description: RRP absorbs QT painlessly while positive (free phase). Once RRP → 0, QT directly drains reserves (painful phase). The transition is a nonlinear cliff, not gradual. Who buys the reissued Treasuries determines what drains: MMFs buy bills → RRP drains. Banks buy coupons → reserves drain.

concepts.label: ETF Creation/Redemption Arbitrage
concepts.description: APs (dealer banks) bridge ETF prices to underlying bond basket NAV via creation (premium) and redemption (discount) arbitrage. Funded by same repo/CP infrastructure as everything else. When repo seizes, bridge breaks, ETF discounts blow out. March 2020: LQD/HYG 3-5% discounts.

concepts.label: Money Market Safety Hierarchy
concepts.description: Safest → riskiest: Fed RRP → Treasury repo → T-bills → Agency DNs → Financial CP → Non-fin CP → ABCP → CDs → Uninsured deposits → Prime MMF shares. In stress, investors flight-to-quality UP the hierarchy, starving the bottom. Hierarchy inversion = crisis signal.

concepts.label: Conversion Factor Reference Yield
concepts.description: CF reference yield is the single design parameter that creates all optionality. At ref yield: all bonds equally cheap to deliver → maximum switch option. Far from ref: one bond dominates → minimal optionality → contract degrades to single-bond forward. 8%→6% change (2000) restored optionality.

concepts.label: Basis Asymmetric Payoff (Short Vol)
concepts.description: Selling basis: capped upside (BNOC earned slowly via theta), uncapped downside (realized suddenly via CTD switch). Buying basis: inverts this (capped loss, uncapped gain). Win rate ~23/25 quarters but losses can dwarf accumulated gains. Every basis trade IS a volatility position.

concepts.label: FX Expectation Formation
concepts.description: Agents forecasting apparatus: base factors (interest rates, macro growth, trade balance, liquidity preference) feed into processes, filtered through indicators. Short-term expectations update rapidly; medium-term attractor (bullish/bearish/neutral) acts as lens that magnifies confirming signals and dampens contradicting ones. Attractor shifts via moving average of accumulated short-run data.

subsumes.label: UIP Failure / Interest Rate Parity Breakdown

concepts.label: FX Bandwagon / Momentum Feedback
concepts.description: Self-reinforcing: appreciation attracts more capital inflows, medium-term attractor becomes pro-currency, confirming signals magnified, contradicting ones ignored. More powerful than equity momentum because no fundamental valuation anchor constrains FX. Breaks when confidence erodes, profit-taking overwhelms, or exogenous shock reverses sentiment.

concepts.label: Currency Crisis Three-Tension-Point Model
concepts.description: Three simultaneous break points accumulate during booms: (1) Currency-forecast divergence: AER vs mental model prediction diverges. (2) Financial-returns divergence: financial asset returns far exceed real economic growth. (3) Financial fragility: debt/income ratios, maturity mismatch (short-term), locational mismatch (foreign-denominated). Any ONE can snap and trigger crisis. Mexican 1994 = TP1+TP3. Asian 1997 = TP2+TP3.

concepts.label: Delivery Options (EOM + Timing + Wild Card)
concepts.description: Residual delivery options beyond the switch option. End-of-month: after futures stop trading, BPV (not duration) determines CTD for 7 business days -- high-coupon bonds can become CTD post-expiry, reversing normal ordering (strangle payoff). Timing: positive carry -> deliver last day; negative carry -> tension between stopping bleed vs preserving remaining option value. Wild card: 3-6hr window after 2PM settlement fix; break-even = Basis/(Factor-1). Hierarchy: switch >> EOM >> wild card >> timing >> limit move.

concepts.label: Corporate Governance / Control / M&A
concepts.description: Every company has status quo value and optimal value. The gap, weighted by probability of management change, is the control premium (voting vs non-voting spread reflects this). Badly-run mature companies have highest control value. In practice, 55-83% of acquisitions destroy acquirer value via risk transference, debt subsidies, phantom synergies, and control premium overpayment. Target shareholders capture all surplus. Acquisition addiction sets in.

When debt/GDP high, issuance composition dominates monetary policy

Coupon supply vs buyer demand clears via term premium adjustment
When investment rises relative to savings, yield sensitivity to debt increases
Protecting bonds devalues currency; protecting currency crashes bonds
Tail shape determines optimal option structure; own convexity when tails mispriced

Uncertainty freezes hiring and capex even before policy materializes
Bill-heavy issuance + Fed RMPs = stealth Operation Twist compressing long-end
JGB moves transmit 2-3bp per 10bp shock to global long-end yields
AI productivity gains are structurally disinflationary over time

Rising IV causes vanna-driven selling that destabilizes markets
VRP is the systematic tendency for implied to exceed realized; gamma-theta contest is the daily expression
Index vol embeds both component vol and correlation; steep skew reflects crash correlation dynamics
Geometric < arithmetic by 0.5*variance; high vol destructive to compounded wealth
Known events systematically overpriced in IV; fade front-end around scheduled events
Dealer hedging obligations form hidden liquidity layer; the implied order book
Put-call parity connects all option prices; cost of carry embedded in the surface
Option prices set by risk-neutral measure; best-capitalized player sets marginal price
Extreme positioning creates fragile equilibrium; pain trade probability rises
Forced liquidation spikes realized vol
Correlation break is the earliest observable signal of regime change
When price and narrative diverge, trust price; narrative eventually capitulates
CA deficit = capital inflow. US as 60% of global deficit = structural asset demand.
Post-GFC shift from bank paper to equities explains S&P outperformance
Commodities drive dollar (not reverse). Commodity up -> EM earnings -> capital to EM -> USD down
Weak dollar raises dollar-denominated commodity prices (reinforcing loop)
Credit drives FX indirectly: credit -> GDP -> asset prices -> FX
Surplus countries must export capital; cannot easily pivot to consumption-led growth
Private + government + foreign balance = 0; deficit spending creates private savings
US-China interdependence makes tariffs the nuclear button; escalation risks mutual recession
Risk-off with negative equity-Treasury correlation pushes yields down
10-year reinforcing cycle: commodities -> EM earnings -> flows -> USD -> commodities
Credit supply freezing = crisis (defend). No freeze = panic (buy the dip).
50-100x provisioning spike in secured lending triggers credit supply freeze
Reserve scarcity pockets spike repo rates, forcing asset selling
Emergency tools normalize -> vol crushed -> short-vol strategies accumulate
Crowded short-vol creates fragile equilibrium; violent blow-up when catalyst arrives
Ponzi finance collapse triggers forced deleveraging and credit contraction
Exhausted stabilizers convert hidden risk into visible crisis
Vol reflexivity: short vol selling -> lower realized vol -> more selling -> lower vol
Lower vol -> more leverage -> higher prices -> buybacks -> lower vol (self-feeding)
Whether 60/40 works depends on CB inflation attitude. Not physics.
High inflation tail -> CB constrained -> bonds fail to hedge equities -> positive correlation
Deceptively high win rates of short vol attract crowding; tail risk hidden in expectancy
Duration extension forces hedgers to pay swaps; swap rates rise faster than Tsy; spreads widen
Extension spiral: rates up -> prepay down -> MBS extends -> hedgers sell -> rates up more
Carry = coupon - financing; rolldown from curve aging. Break-even = (carry+rolldown)/duration
Different buyers dominate different tenors; structural shifts move specific curve sectors
Strong auctions validate demand; weak auctions temporarily widen yields. Self-correcting.
Duration = first order, convexity = second order. In large moves, convexity dominates P&L.
Basis = cash vs futures. BNOC = delivery option value. Futures cheap by option premium.
10Y oscillates in range until catalyst. Bollinger compression signals imminent break.
On-the-run bonds on special in repo -> lower financing cost -> enhanced carry
Higher ERP = stocks cheaper. ERP near 4.25% = fair value. Below 2.5% = bubble.
Excess returns exist only with moats. 70%+ of firms earn below cost of capital.
Overconfident entrepreneurs + VCs inflate valuations; collectively a fairy tale
Time and volatility keep equity alive even at zero intrinsic value
Every capex boom ends in glut; picks and shovels work until they dont
TGA drawdown + bank loan creation = short-term liquidity signal for equities
Dealer balance sheet -> financing cost vs SOFR -> hard ceiling on risk appetite
QE is NOT printing (asset swap). Fiscal deficits and CB equity depletion are.
Rising gold may signal falling trust in financial system; shift toward pricing-power assets
Rising IV compresses risk appetite, triggers dealer hedging, destabilizes spot prices
Inelastic collar roll flow creates predictable local turning points. Structural periodic mechanism.
Collar roll shifts gamma exposure at specific strikes, temporarily altering the dealer hedging landscape
Foreign selling of USTs reduces structural demand for duration; term premium rises
Capital diversification away from US assets weakens the dollar simultaneously with yield rise
Rebalancing drag on levered ETFs is a direct expression of volatility drain (0.5*variance cost)
Market price = status quo + P(change) * (optimal - status quo). Governance reform in EM -> badly-run companies jump most.
55-83% of acquisitions destroy acquirer value. Phantom synergies, risk transference, overpayment for control.
Passive removes active vol buffers. Each buyer/seller amplifies. Tail risk rises as passive share grows.
Flow-driven markets increase importance of dealer hedging as the marginal price-setting mechanism
Credit regime deterioration leads vol regime shift. Track solvency and credit conditions to forecast vol.
Credit regime is the exogenous shock channel that can break the Ouroboros suppression loop
FICC netting reduces balance sheet cost of intermediation. Expands effective dealer capacity.
Sponsored repo via FICC allows non-members to access central clearing. Enables basis trade growth.
The pyramid IS the body of the Ouroboros. Explicit + implicit + structural layers = $2T+ short vol.
Preemptive CB action compresses realized vol via Pavlovian mean reversion. Markets embed rescue expectation.
Suppressed price vol transmutes into hidden tail risk and social instability (populism). Cancer patient gets amphetamines.
Moral hazard loop: CB rescue -> more risk-taking -> lower vol -> CB points to success -> more accommodation
The Serpent IS the Ouroboros regime. Secular growth, moral hazard, reflexive self-cannibalization.
Serpent = negative correlation (60/40 works). Hawk right wing (inflation) = positive correlation (60/40 fails).
Sell first-order vol carry (term structure roll, uncertainty premia) to finance higher-order convexity. Makes long vol sustainable.
Conditions (leverage, crowding, liquidity, valuations) determine whether any spark becomes conflagration or brush fire.
Low persistence: sell vol at 30 works, mean reverts fast. High persistence: vol stays elevated for years, short vol bankrupts.
Inflation constrains CB -> Fed put invalidated -> vol autocorrelation rises -> regime switches from low to high persistence.
Updrafts prevent the Fed from cutting as fast as growth warrants. Each sticky print delays normalization.
Must classify asset as mean-reverting or trending BEFORE interpreting positioning. Overbought in trend = bullish, in range = bearish.
Crypto turns first (no valuation anchor, pure positioning). Equities follow with lag. Shitcoin rallies = peak FOMO.
G4+ divergence creates cross-market rate spread opportunities. Front-end divergence persists; long-end bounded by correlated global TP.
Front-end = CB path. Belly = terminal + convexity. Long-end = risk premium + supply + cross-border. Different forces, different timescales.
Variable-rate economies (UK, AUS, CA) crack first from rate hikes. US insulated by fixed-rate mortgages. CB divergence trade.
Rate sensitivity divergence across economies creates FX mean-reversion opportunities when weak-link countries forced to cut early.
The ratio is (carry + rolldown) / (duration x yield vol); the proper risk-adjusted metric. Not carry alone.
High carry-to-vol attracts herding into same trades; positions saturate; months of carry erased in days when unwind hits
Carry crowding manifests as cross-market carry correlation spike and positioning buildup toward historical extremes
Muted response to favorable news + outsized to unfavorable = crowded carry long (no marginal buyer left, many potential sellers)
Successive exit waves -> vol spikes -> carry/vol collapses -> months of income erased in days. 2009 front-end carry unwind template.
Z = (current - mean) / vol standardizes RV attractiveness across instruments. Best RV trades combine: cheapness + positive carry + catalyst.
After factor decomposition, residual = pure RV component; trade the residual via Z-score framework, not the raw butterfly
Same spread; completely different dominant factor depending on regime. Multi-factor analysis becomes irrelevant during systemic events.
Mortgage extension is the dominant short-term swap spread driver; can persist for months. 2003: yields +150bp, spreads +40bp.
Demographics: pension demand for ultra-long fixed to match liabilities. Swaps preferred over Treasuries: unlimited size, no auction dependence.
Arb doesnt close because it requires bank balance sheet capacity-constrained by SLR. Structural mispricing persists.
Carry component is deterministic, lockable via term repo. BNOC is stochastic with option-like decay. Implied repo connects both.
Duration rule (yield level) + yield rule (yield spread) determine CTD. Two orthogonal forces operating simultaneously.
Spread-yield correlation saturates -> switch option killed -> CTD permanently entrenched -> basis trading dies. 1991-93 template.
BNOC collapses to carry + trivial EOM option. Negative convexity zone vanishes. Recovery requires exogenous correlation break.
OI exceeds deliverable float -> BNOC goes negative -> market transitions from option-pricing to failure-probability regime.
CTD goes special in repo -> RP rate spirals toward 0% or negative -> delivery failure becomes probable.
Paradox: most attractive short basis = greatest squeeze risk. Crowd positioning creates the scarcity it seeks to exploit. Reflexive.
OAB = observed BNOC - theoretical delivery option value. Persistent OAB < 0 = structural selling pressure from mortgage hedgers.
Cross-checks basis model against listed options market. Model-implied futures call should match exchange option price; calibrate vol.
10Y futures chronically cheap due to structural mortgage hedging selling pressure. Primary enhancement vehicle for decades.
Conditional spread trades require directional beta (R²) between spread and level. In regimes where beta breaks, conditionality adds nothing.
Hedge choice converts outright rate risk to spread/basis risk. Swap hedges track credit in stress; Treasury hedges fail in FTQ.
Treasury hedges fail in FTQ: risky bond loses (spread widens) AND Treasury short loses (Treasury rallies). Both legs lose simultaneously.
Structural flows create persistent richness/cheapness at specific vol surface points; vol spread trades exploit these kinks.
GSE demand supports intermediate vol; callable issuance depresses long-end; range accruals compress cap vol vs swaption vol.
Mortgage portfolio hedging = primary source of intermediate-tail swaption demand (3Yx10Y rich due to GSE buying).
Shadow money = outstanding x price x (1-haircut) x velocity. Three multiplicative factors: small moves in each compound into massive effective liquidity swings.
Velocity contraction = invisible monetary shock. Does not appear in any traditional aggregate. 10-14 large dealers control global rehypothecation.
Haircuts compress in booms (amplify effective liquidity), spike in crises (contract it). Haircut cycle LEADS credit cycle.
Haircut cycle parallels and leads Minsky progression. Easy terms -> speculative -> Ponzi -> haircut spike triggers forced deleveraging.
When balance sheets constrained, spreads widen, intermediation capacity shrinks. Liquidity exists but cant flow to where needed.
Tail value depends entirely on prevailing short rate at refinancing date. Term repo eliminates carry uncertainty; tail remains exposed.
OTR holders earn lending fee = (GC - specials rate) x notional. Owning OTR = carry trade + specials income stream. Separate P/L channel.
Specialness is predictable (auction cycle) and persistent; decays as bond ages through lifecycle from OTR to deep off-the-run.
New auction creates new OTR, demoting current. OTR richness cycle: post-auction richening -> pre-next-auction cheapening.
Rich (OTR, special, tight bid-ask) -> compresses (first off-the-run) -> cheap (deep off-the-run, wide bid-ask, held by buy-and-hold).
Futures have OPPOSITE convexity to bonds: duration increases as yields rise, decreases as yields fall. Second-order effect dominates in large moves.
CTD shifts to higher duration on yield rise (amplifies loss); to lower duration on yield fall (curtails gain). Source of negative convexity.
FHLB advances boost LCR: proceeds = L1 HQLA (reserves), FHLB advance = secured (low outflow rate). Only FHLBs provide net-positive LCR boost.
LCR gaming at month-end: banks borrow from FHLBs to inflate snapshot. Category IV banks especially aggressive. Unwind next day.
LCR boosting increases FHLB advance activity → FHLBs have more cash to lend FF → temporary upward EFFR pressure at reporting dates
Park-vs-deploy decision determines exactly where EFFR settles. All park → EFFR at FHLB rate (~IORB-8bp). Deploy → EFFR tighter toward IORB.
FBOs are downstream of FHLBs: borrow FF at IORB-8bp, park at Fed (risk-free 8bp) or deploy into GCF/DVP/FX swaps (higher spread, more risk)
When FBOs deploy into repos instead of parking at Fed, more cash enters repo market → tighter SOFR. Deploy decision directly affects secured funding cost.
FX swap deployment: highest spread (+50bp in stress) but increases G-SIB cross-jurisdictional score. SLR-efficient (off-BS), G-SIB-costly. Near year-end, FBOs cut FX swaps.
Stable hierarchy: specials < bills < triparty < GCF < SOFR < EFFR < IORB < SRF. Spread relationships encode real-time information about system health.
IORB is gravitational center. Banks step in above IORB. SOFR/EFFR orbit below. Headwinds push rates up; tailwinds push down. Fed operational tools maintain sweet spot.
Sweet spot = rates comfortably between floor (RRP) and anchor (IORB). Fed adjusts administered rates, conducts ad hoc repos, adjusts QT pace to maintain.
Effective money = M2 + shadow money. Traditional M2 understates true monetary stock by trillions. Shadow money created endogenously by private collateral markets.
Effective shadow liquidity = shadow money x velocity. 2007: $10T received / $3.3T source = velocity 3.0. 2010: velocity 2.4. Invisible monetary contraction.
QE = public shadow money expansion (asset swap, not printing). Fiscal deficits = net new public money. Both substitute for contracted private shadow money.
RRP absorbs QT painlessly while positive (free phase). MMFs buy bills instead of RRP. Once RRP → 0, cliff: every dollar of QT drains reserves directly.
After RRP drained, QT takes reserves. Distribution matters: pockets of scarcity even if aggregate adequate. Sept 2019 template.
Post-RRP phase: reserve scarcity → repo rate spikes above IORB → settlement fails rise → matched book spreads widen → Fed must intervene.
Who buys determines what drains. MMFs buy bills → RRP drains (painless). Banks buy coupons → reserves drain (contractionary). Treasury issuance mix is critical.
AP activity consumes dealer BS. When BS constrained: APs cant fund arb → ETF discounts blow out. March 2020: LQD/HYG 3-5% discounts for days.
ETF liquidity = derivative of repo/CP health. When repo seizes, AP bridge breaks, price discovery in bond ETFs fails. Self-reinforcing in stress.
AP creation/redemption aligns ETF prices to underlying bonds. When functioning: liquidity transmission. When broken: price discovery fails.
MMFs buy FHLB discount notes as investment. FHLBs receive cash, lend FF to FBOs. MMFs are the ultimate cash source; FHLBs the intermediary; FBOs the deployers.
Universal netting via FICC reduces matched book BS cost → more dealer capacity → tighter spreads → lower repo rates for end users.
Non-diversifiable concentration risk. BNY extends hundreds of billions intraday during 3:30 PM pivot. Central clearing expansion increases FICC criticality further.
Asymmetric corridor: RRP is stigma-free hard floor (MMFs use willingly). SRF is stigma-impaired soft ceiling. Holds at bottom, leaks at top.
Bills yield below repo because captive demand (regulated investors cannot repo). Spread encodes access constraints. Widens in flight-to-safety.
When bill-repo spread widens: MMFs prefer repo/RRP. When narrows: MMFs buy bills. Spread dynamics determine MMF allocation path.
EFFR targeting legacy: thin ($80-100B daily), FHLB-dominated, declining relevance. SOFR now >$1T daily. Future: FICC-cleared rate most representative.
Central clearing enables transition to cleared repo rate as primary benchmark. Most transparent, nettable, representative. Structural shift.
G-SIB + SLR = dual constraint. SLR binds for repo (no risk-weighting). G-SIB binds for FX swaps (cross-jurisdictional) and year-end balance sheet.
FX swaps: SLR-efficient (off-BS) but G-SIB-costly (cross-jurisdictional). Near year-end, FBOs/dealers cut FX swaps → widen cross-currency basis.
Dealers structurally long-biased → persistent front-end long bid. Shorting requires buyer AND source. Only govies/agencies homogeneous enough for size.
Reversing preferred over borrowing for shorts. Asymmetry means market has permanent long bias. This is structural, not a signal.
FBO deployment arithmetic: park earns IORB (above FF). Deploy into repo (above FF cost). Repo-FF spread is part of the deployment P/L calculation.
Hierarchy from Fed RRP (riskless) through Treasury repo through T-bills down to uninsured deposits. In stress, investors flight UP the hierarchy, starving the bottom.
Stable hierarchy = functioning plumbing (panic, recoverable). Hierarchy inversion (instruments breaking rank order) = crisis signal. March 2020 briefly inverted.
TGA drawdown is mirror of QT: injects reserves. TGA drawdown > QT pace → net reserve addition. Debt ceiling episodes create massive injections.
When effective money (M2 + shadow) grows faster than productive capacity, excess claims reconcile via price adjustment. Traditional M2 understates true monetary expansion.
Public expansion (QE, T-bills, Fed facilities) substitutes for collapsed private shadow money. If substitution insufficient → deflationary spiral.
Futures price = min(CF-adjusted forward prices). Distance from any bonds converted forward to envelope = that bonds BNOC. CTD sits on envelope; non-CTDs above.
After futures stop trading, invoice locked but cash moves 7 days. BPV (not duration) determines CTD post-expiry. Prevents BNOC from reaching zero at expiration.
BPV vs duration paradox: high-coupon bonds (high BPV, low duration) can become CTD post-expiry, reversing normal ordering. Strangle payoff on tails.
Single design parameter. At ref yield: all bonds equally cheap → max switch option. Far from ref: one dominates → contract degrades to single-bond forward.
Short CTD basis P/L = -Δ(BNOC). Exact for CTD. Carry deterministic, nets out. Stochastic P/L is entirely BNOC change. 23/25 quarters profitable (10Y).
True carry ≠ screen carry by the RP/RRP friction. CTD identification should use (IRR - own RP rate), not just IRR. Real carry differs from quoted carry.
Squeeze widens calendar spread: shorts forced to roll (buy front, sell back). Non-CTD bases compress as CTD richens. Calendar spread is the squeeze pressure gauge.
OTR goes special post-auction → basis widens → short hedges accumulate → more specialness → richening reinforces. Then reverses pre-next-auction.
Selling basis = selling vol. Capped upside (BNOC) earned slowly; uncapped downside realized suddenly. Classic short-vol signature identical to all vol-selling strategies.
Basis selling = short vol. Win 23/25 quarters draws crowding. Same deceptive high win rate as all short-vol strategies. Tail risk hidden in expectancy.
Mortgage hedging structural selling keeps 10Y futures chronically cheap. Vol arbs slowly close gap but structural flows maintain cheapness for years. Golden age regime.
Basis trade (long cash, short futures, repo-financed) creates concentrated positioning. Mass unwind → Treasury market stress → systemic (March 2020). Leverage amplifies.
FICC netting allows basis trade growth → leverage demand concentrates in fewer CTDs. Central clearing enables the crowding that eventually creates squeeze risk.
RP/RRP spread = dealers matched book compensation for basis trade intermediation. When specials spike, short basis sellers pay; dealers intermediating earn.
BNOC decay rate (theta) vs expansion risk (gamma) maps to carry-to-vol framework. Short basis carry/vol must incorporate BNOC tail risk, not just income/duration vol.
When ref yield close to market: multiple CTDs, active optionality, basis ecosystem alive. 6% change (2000) restored basis trading, vol arb, AND synthetic enhancement.
Quarter-end window dressing interacts with OTR cycle: dealers shrink repo → OTR specials intensify → basis widens → then reverses post-quarter-end.
Calendar spread = front - back. Components: carry differential + CTD difference + delivery option difference. Enhanced basis via delivery captures 2-3/32nds discount.
Grid uses yield betas (nonparallel shifts). Worst case: curve steepening in selloff. Betas from calm periods may not hold in stress → grid understates tail risk.
Repo DV01 ≈ $2.88/$100k vs Spot DV01 ≈ $147. Independent weekly. But repo dislocations (Sept 2019) create unexpected basis P/L via the repo channel.
3-day delivery: tender → notice → delivery. 2PM close to 5-8PM notice = wild card window. Bonds-in-the-box: custody 2 days before delivery alters last-mile carry.
Three drivers connect to vol surface: yield vol from swaptions, curve slope from term structure, nonsystematic from specific issue dynamics. Basis-implied vol vs listed vol.
Leveraged basis unwind (March 2020) = panic if credit intact, crisis if credit freezes. Basis trade unwind is the primary positioning flush mechanism in rates markets.
Futures at min of converted prices → dealer hedging tracks whichever bond cheapens fastest. Negative convexity forces dynamic rehedging analogous to option delta adjustment.
New issuance replenishes or shifts deliverable set. High-coupon new issue at high yields → low duration → NOT CTD when high-duration needed. Paradoxical interaction.
EOM option prevents BNOC from reaching zero at expiry → basis sellers always give up some residual premium. Bounds the maximum convergence gain for leveraged basis traders.
Coupon issuance is the primary supply-side driver at each tenor. 10Y+ needs structurally higher than structural demand.
QRA includes TGA target. TGA policy floor is dynamic (1-week forward outflows). QRA sets the path.
Risk premium is the price of risk. Low risk premiums stimulate regardless of rate levels. Financial conditions = composite of risk premiums across assets.
Higher risk premium → lower asset prices (higher discount rate). Low risk premiums = certainty premiums justifying higher multiples.
P/E inversely related to risk premium. Certainty premiums (government backstops) justify structurally higher multiples.
Earnings events create kinks in vol term structure. Pre-event: IV ramps. Post-event: vol crush. Event vol extraction trades this.
Value = PV of expected cash flows. Earnings are NOT cash flows. Profits proxy value only after subtracting reinvestment needs.
Long rate is the discount rate for all cash flows. Higher long yields → lower PV → lower intrinsic value. Bond market IS everything.
ERP solved backward from market prices + expected cash flows. Far superior to PE ratios. Best single gauge of market valuation.
Without moats, growth destroys value (reinvestment > returns). Only moated companies earn excess returns. 70%+ of firms earn below CoC.
Reverse-engineer from market cap what revenue/margins must deliver. Apply 3P test (possible, plausible, probable). Discipline for narrative.
Pricing = what others will pay (multiples, relative). Valuation = what its worth (DCF, absolute). ERP bridges both: solved from market prices but measures intrinsic cheapness.
Asset prices reflect pricing (flow of funds, positioning, narrative) not valuation. Different questions, can give opposite signals.
ASW deviation from own history signals richness/cheapness. Z-score standardization essential. Best RV trades: cheapness + carry + catalyst.
Futures ASW keeps trade in derivatives space (no repo needed). ASW is the carry-adjusted RV measure for specific bonds.
OTR bonds trade rich (negative ASW). Off-the-run trade cheap (positive ASW). ASW tracks the lifecycle richness decay.
Term premium, not fed funds, determines actual tightness. Financial conditions = composite of risk premiums. The stance of policy works through the long end.
Tighter financial conditions → lower asset prices. Looser → higher. Composite of rates, spreads, equity prices, dollar.
Financial conditions transmit to real economy via wealth effect (top 10% drives 50% of consumption). Tighter → lower wealth → lower spending.
Financial conditions → asset prices → wealth effect → consumption → nominal demand. The full transmission chain.
When debt/GDP high, bills-vs-coupons decision is more impactful than fed funds rate. Treasury Secretary controls the real monetary policy lever.
Fiscal dominance emerges when Omega is high. Deficit is the engine, rates are the steering wheel. Issuance flows dominate monetary policy.
Fiscal dominance means yields respond more to issuance composition than to Fed. Coupon-heavy → yields up. Bill-heavy → neutral.
High debt/GDP makes bond market instability more likely → fear policy activates more frequently. Each foam delays but increases eventual correction.
Policy intervention prevents disorderly long-end selloff. Dovish pivot, bill shift, BTFP → yields stabilize. Keeps term premium compressed.
Foam the runway → yields stabilize → equity multiples recover → wealth effect re-heats economy. Each intervention resets asset prices higher.
Foam prevents demand destruction. Wealth effect recovers. Top 10% continues consuming. Inflation re-heats. Higherer for Longerer.
Income-driven economy: short rates ineffective (fixed-rate mortgages insulate). Need long yields / asset prices to slow demand. Different channel.
Post-Covid economy runs on income + wealth, not credit. Strong wages + asset appreciation → consumption continues despite rate hikes.
Protecting bonds = fear policy's revealed preference. US has always chosen to protect bonds → sacrifices currency → inflation persists.
Protecting bonds devalues currency. Gold hedges the 'protect bonds' outcome. Rising gold signals policy choosing bonds over currency.
Currency devaluation from bond protection triggers foreign 'Get Out'. Yields up + USD down simultaneously signals foreign seller.
Higher long yields compress ERP (stocks must compete with bonds). ERP near 4.25% = fair value. Below 2.5% = bubble.
ERP = cost of equity - risk-free rate. Solved backward from market prices. DCF uses ERP as discount rate input.
Excess returns exist only with moats. Width (ROIC-WACC) and depth (duration). DCF value >> current earnings only if moats persist.
Daily P/L = 0.5*Gamma*(Move)^2 - Theta*dt. Maps directly: carry (theta) vs vol (gamma). The fundamental options trade-off IS carry-to-vol.
Short basis = short gamma (earns theta/carry slowly, bleeds on moves). Long basis = long gamma. Same contest.
Implied vol = breakeven daily move to overcome theta for long gamma position. If realized > implied, long gamma wins.
C = (S-K) + P + cost_of_carry. Equity = call on assets. Debt = risk-free - put on assets. Merton capital structure from parity.
Crashes: both vol AND correlation spike (multiplicative). Index vol = avg stock vol x avg correlation. Basket of options > option on basket.
High correlation → index vol spikes beyond single-stock vol sum. Dispersion trades exploit correlation premium between index and components.
Private + government + foreign balance = 0. Deficit spending creates private savings (identity). One sectors deficit = anothers surplus.
CA deficit = capital inflow (identity). US as 60% of global deficit = structural demand for US assets from surplus countries.
DSR = interest payments / income. Must stay tolerable (~10-15%). When Omega rises, yields MUST fall to keep DSR bearable. Forces yields down.
High DSR chokes off new borrowing. Constraint on credit expansion at any given rate level.
When aggregate DSR high, Fed must cut eventually regardless of inflation. Debt service constraint overrides inflation mandate.
When investment rises relative to savings (AI capex, China surplus shifts), yield sensitivity to public debt increases. More supply, less absorption.
Global savings-investment balance determines who finances whom. Chinas surplus and AI capex are the two largest structural forces.
5Y5Y forward reacts to, never propels, the cycle. But central banks watch it. Unanchoring would force aggressive tightening.
Inflation expectations are one component of nominal yields (Fisher equation). But empirically, credit pipeline dominates.
Uncertainty freezes hiring and capex even before policy materializes. No hire, no fire stall speed. Markets discount paralysis.
Uncertainty → investment freeze → revenue growth stalls → margin pressure. The damage comes before the policy, not after.
Bill-heavy issuance + Fed RMPs = stealth Operation Twist. Both institutions want lower long-end yields. Coordination is implicit.
Bessant Issuance Doctrine: Treasury reluctant to increase long-end coupon sizes. Shift to bills = implicit coordination with Fed.
Dammit Janet: Treasury Secretary controls the real monetary policy lever via issuance composition. Fed-Treasury coordination IS the fear policy mechanism.
Bank loan creation is the private component. TGA drawdown is the public component. Together = total new money entering the system.
TIPS dominated by liquidity premiums, not inflation expectations. Gold and equity multiples provide cleaner real-rate signals.
Equity multiples reflect real discount rates more cleanly than TIPS (no liquidity premium distortion). P/E as real-rate signal.
USD not rallying on tariffs = correlation break. When a correlation that should hold doesnt, something structural has changed. Earliest regime-change tell.
Wages follow prices with 1-quarter lag. Wages → income → consumption → demand. Adaptive, not forward-looking.
Must classify asset as mean-reverting or trending BEFORE interpreting positioning. Overbought in trend = bullish continuation. Overbought in range = contrarian fade.
Phase of narrative determines whether sentiment is contrarian or confirmatory. Phase 3 consensus: contrarian for ranges. Phase 2 adoption: momentum for trends.
Crypto turns lower while QQQ chugs → equities follow with lag. No fundamental valuation constraint → pure positioning barometer for risk appetite.
Shitcoin rallies = peak retail FOMO. BTC vol declining = no marginal buyer. Leverage indicators (MSTR, 2X ETFs) = reflexive loops that will break.
Every capex boom ends in glut. Revenue/capex ratio tolerance flips from infinite to zero overnight. Management forecasts to 2030 = red flag.
AI capex follows same arc: boom → shortage → overbuilding → glut. Short-run reflationary (wealth effect from AI equity valuations). Long-run deflationary (productivity).
When price and narrative diverge, trust price. What matters is not WHY but WHAT the market is doing. Narrative capitulates to price, not reverse.
Yields up + USD down simultaneously = foreign seller, not domestic repricing. The correlation break encodes the capital flow shift underneath.
Negative correlation: bonds hedge equities, financial conditions have natural dampener. Positive: both fall together, conditions tighten via both channels simultaneously.
CB inflation constraint determines correlation. When CB cannot cut → stocks AND bonds fall → 60/40 impaired → nowhere to hide except cash. CB paralysis = positive correlation.
Fed Put since 1987: stocks up 74% of years (vs 64% pre-Put). CB backstop raises sustainable equity valuations via certainty premium.
Inflation hits bottom 90% harder via food and energy. Top 10% less affected (services spending). Aggregate inflation masks distributional pain.
Right tail (higher yields) vs left tail (lower yields). Distribution shape matters more than point forecast. Own convexity when tails are mispriced.
The distribution of rate outcomes matters more than the point forecast. Inflation persistence is the gatekeeper of right-tail shape.
Bollinger band compression signals imminent move. IV declines during consolidation as realized vol compresses. Break releases stored energy.
Duration = first order, convexity = second order. DV01 = modified duration × price × 0.0001. In large moves convexity dominates P/L.
Break-even yield rise = (carry + rolldown) / duration. Duration converts yield to price. Duration is the denominator in the carry-to-vol ratio.
Surplus countries must export capital → structural demand for USD assets. Trap: they cannot diversify without triggering their own crisis.
10-year cycle: commodities up → EM earnings → capital to EM → USD down → commodities up (reinforcing). Reversal: vicious cycle in other direction.
High win rate ≠ positive expectancy. Short vol/basis wins most months but loses catastrophically. Slugging percentage matters, not batting average.
VVIX divergence from VIX is a leading indicator of vol regime shift. Vol-of-vol moves first → implied vol follows → spot follows. Composed indicator.
60/40 covers 2 regimes (serpent: growth + deflation). Dragon covers all 4 including inflation and squeeze. Equal thematic weight, lever to vol target. No prediction required.
SLOOS tightening leads credit contraction by ~12 months. Loosening leads expansion. The survey IS the leading indicator of private credit growth.
Lending standards → credit → spending. Tightening precedes slowdown by 12-18 months. Among the best macro leading indicators.
IORB is the gravitational center of the corridor. EFFR orbits ~8bp below. Banks step in above IORB. All secured rates orbit below it.
SOFR/TGCR/BGCR orbit below IORB. When repos breach above IORB, it signals reserve scarcity (Sept 2019 template). IORB is the anchor.
SRF = theoretical ceiling on repo rates (upper bound of target). But stigma makes it soft — dealers prefer paying above-SRF. Fed supplements with ad hoc TOMOs.
Daily withholding receipts = real-time labor market signal. Core 20 business day baseline with seasonal overlays. Faster than BLS.
April 15 tax surge, SS payment schedule, GSE remittance cycle, quarter-end. Predictable periodic patterns create tradeable rate distortions.
50-100x provisioning spike in secured lending triggers tightening cascade. Each crisis originates in a NEW safe asset class. Losses in secured, not unsecured, cause shock.
PC usage accumulates against 30% UPB cap. When exhausted, loans re-default, hidden distress becomes visible. Dampener exhaustion pattern → potential provisioning spike.
Japan is primary exporter of bearish long-end shocks. JGB moves transmit 2-3bp per 10bp shock to US/DE/UK. Global long-end views must incorporate Japan.
Regression-based: FX vs rate differential. When residual hits extreme Z-score, mean-reverts. Isolates the non-rate component of FX. Tradeable signal.
Pattern-match: 1928/1987/1998/2008. All had conditions (leverage, crowding, compressed premiums) that made any catalyst sufficient. The conditions ARE the risk, not the spark.
Front-end divergence persists (domestic CB path). Long-end divergence bounded (global risk premium correlated). G4+ divergence creates cross-market rate spread opportunities.
Structural growth/inflation mix differences across Fed/ECB/BOE/BOJ/BOC. Front-end: each CB anchors its own short end. Creates exploitable relative value.
Front-end = CB policy path. Belly = terminal rate + convexity. Long-end = risk premium + supply + cross-border. Different forces, different timescales.
Normal bp vol ≈ lognormal % vol × forward rate. Near zero: lognormal explodes. Wrong convention → severe mispricing. Rates: normal. Equities: lognormal.
Normal (Bachelier) model for rates: vol in absolute bp/yr. Rates move in absolute terms (25bp hike regardless of level). Essential for swaption/cap pricing.
Portfolio capital flows, not trade, are the primary driver of exchange rates. 40x trade volume. Dominant over ALL time horizons.
Capital dominance means one price cannot clear two markets; AER diverges from BTER persistently.
Mental model forecasts drive portfolio capital allocation decisions across currencies.
Medium-term attractor amplifies confirming signals and dampens contradicting ones, feeding bandwagon.
Bandwagon pushes currency beyond any fundamental anchor. Self-reinforcing until confidence breaks.
Bandwagon-supported overvaluation builds tension at all three crisis break points simultaneously.
When TP3 (financial fragility) snaps, secured lending losses and provisioning spike can trigger credit freeze.
Import cost inflation feeds domestic prices in nations with inelastic trade and no domestic substitutes.
Rising Minsky fragility in open economy loads all three tension points; each boom pushes agents toward Ponzi finance.
FX indicator rotation is the currency-market-specific instance of Donnellys narrative regime rotation.
Capital account is the dominant, independent side of the BOP identity. Current account accommodates.
Persistent directional biases create herding and consensus positioning in FX via shared mental model framework.
Overextended FX bandwagons create fragile positioning; reversal triggers VAR shock and forced unwind.
Three-tension framework discriminates crisis severity. All three snapping = full crisis. One only = potentially recoverable.
Uncertainty erodes confidence in mental model forecasts; agents withdraw or shorten time horizons.
Dollar forecast drives portfolio flows to/from US. Mental model attractor (pro/anti-dollar) is the dominant determinant.
Global rate shocks can trigger or break FX bandwagons via interest rate differential channel.
EM debt buildup exhausts loss-absorption buffers (foreign reserves, CB capacity) before crisis materializes.
Trade-based FX theories work only when capital flows choose to focus on trade. Otherwise the 10yr commodity cycle operates via capital, not goods, channels.
UIP failure is what creates the exploitable FX rate residual. The non-rate component of FX is the mental model premium.
EM nations in spiral become more dependent on surplus-country financing, deepening the trap.
Profit-taking during strong trends mimics the good-news-bad-price signal but is mechanical, not fundamental.
Funding stress raises required risk premium for equities. Repo dislocation transmits to equity risk appetite via dealer balance sheet.
Coupon-heavy issuance raises the long-end discount rate, mechanically reducing PV of all cash flows in DCF.
Constrained dealers reduce market-making capacity, widen spreads, and reduce ability to absorb risk, raising effective ERP.
Large FX moves spill into equity vol via multinational earnings translation, FX carry trade unwinds, and cross-asset risk-off.
FX bandwagon reversals create cross-asset vol spikes; skew steepens as tail risk reprices across markets simultaneously.
Strong dollar compresses EM asset correlations (all suffer together); weak dollar allows dispersion across EM.
Credit expansion suppresses realized vol which pulls implied vol lower. Credit contraction spikes both. Credit-vol brotherhood.
SLOOS tightening predicts shift from low-persistence (CB rescues, mean-revert) to high-persistence (CB constrained) vol regime.
Credit expansion feeds the vol-suppression loop: more credit -> more leverage -> lower vol -> more credit. The Ouroboros eats credit.
Rates carry is the Serpent-regime income engine in the Dragon portfolio. Duration carry funds the long-vol allocation.
Term premium level signals which macro regime is active. Rising TP = Hawk approaching. Compressed TP = deep Serpent.
Credit impulse direction indicates which macro regime is active for portfolio construction purposes.
Secular debt/GDP level determines whether Serpent (growth+deflation) or Hawk (inflation+squeeze) regime dominates structurally.
Strong dollar compresses multinational earnings via translation effect. S&P500 earns ~40% revenue abroad.
Currency moves affect cash flow translation AND discount rates for international firms. Dual channel.
Foreign selling of US assets raises ERP via both reduced demand and uncertainty premium from de-dollarization.
Weak auction forces primary dealers to absorb supply, shifts net positioning, and temporarily constrains risk capacity.
Quarter-end dealer balance sheet shrinkage affects hedging capacity, temporarily shifting effective GEX and destabilizing index dynamics.

loops.label: Debt-Asset Reflexivity
loops.constraint: SLOOS tightening constrains private credit expansion
loops.breaks_when: Impulse turns sharply negative (both public AND private contracting) or Omega exceeds saturation threshold

loops.label: Minsky Instability Cycle
loops.constraint: Hedge -> speculative -> Ponzi finance progression
loops.breaks_when: Asset prices stop rising; forced deleveraging begins; Ponzi borrowers cannot refinance

loops.label: TGA Thermostat
loops.constraint: Risk policy floor is DYNAMIC (1-week forward outflows), not static 750B
loops.breaks_when: Debt ceiling binding disables the thermostat; Treasury cannot issue new bills

loops.label: RRP Equilibrium
loops.constraint: MMF WAM limit (~60 days) caps bill purchase capacity; structural RRP floor exists
loops.breaks_when: Bill supply drops (debt ceiling) -> yields below RRP -> MMFs shift back to RRP

loops.label: GEX Stabilizer
loops.constraint: When GEX > $1bn/pt, daily ranges compress to ~0.20%
loops.breaks_when: GEX approaches zero; other factors dominate; VEX takes over

loops.label: Vanna Crash Feedback
loops.constraint: Requires customer-sold puts going ITM
loops.breaks_when: IV peaks; dealer selling exhausts; new buying arrives at lower prices

loops.label: Ouroboros (Vol Self-Cannibalization)
loops.constraint: CB ability to suppress vol via low rates / backstops
loops.breaks_when: Inflation spike -> CB unable to rescue -> vol shock persists -> simultaneous deleveraging (1987 template)

loops.label: Mortgage Extension Spiral
loops.constraint: Rate move magnitude determines extension severity
