loops.breaks_when: Rates move so far that most mortgages deeply OTM -> extension saturates; or swaption hedges absorb convexity

loops.label: Wealth Effect Loop
loops.constraint: Top 10% drives 50% of consumption; loop breaks if equities correct >20%
loops.breaks_when: Sharp equity correction reverses wealth effect -> consumption collapses -> synchronized recession

loops.label: Fear Policy vs The Script
loops.constraint: The Script (correction economy needs) vs Foam the Runway (policy prevents it). Central tension.
loops.breaks_when: Inflation forces CB to not intervene -> The Script plays out -> term premium expansion -> multiple compression -> recession

loops.label: Commodity-Dollar Reinforcing Cycle
loops.constraint: 10-year cycle length
loops.breaks_when: Reversal: commodity bust -> EM outflows -> USD strength -> further commodity weakness

loops.label: Stability Breeds Fragility
loops.constraint: Emergency tools dont eliminate the cycle; change frequency and amplitude
loops.breaks_when: ~2yr calm cadence -> blow-up magnitude proportional to prior calm duration -> capital destruction resets risk premiums

loops.label: Competition Erodes Excess Returns
loops.constraint: 70%+ of global companies earn below cost of capital
loops.breaks_when: One company achieves exclusive capability (temporary moat); disruption resets life cycle

loops.label: Narrative Exhaustion Detector
loops.constraint: Signal strength proportional to degree of consensus positioning; noise if balanced
loops.breaks_when: Narrative is genuinely new (not priced in) -> confirming data produces further trend, not exhaustion

loops.label: Special Repo Self-Reinforcement
loops.constraint: On-the-run bonds attract demand -> lower financing -> more demand
loops.breaks_when: New issue replaces current on-the-run; demand diffuses

loops.label: Credit-Driven Business Cycle
loops.constraint: Each cycle leaves a higher Omega floor (debt ratchet effect)
loops.breaks_when: Minsky moment -> forced deleveraging -> credit contraction -> recession -> emergency tools restart cycle at higher debt level

loops.label: Dealer Balance Sheet Liquidity
loops.constraint: SLR is the binding constraint; nettable vs unnettable repos determine capacity
loops.breaks_when: Quarter-end window dressing, reserve scarcity, or regulatory shock exhausts capacity -> intermediation breaks

loops.label: Carry Crowding Cycle
loops.constraint: High carry-to-vol attracts participants until positioning saturates; cross-market correlation spike is the warning
loops.breaks_when: Small catalyst -> successive exit waves -> vol spikes -> carry/vol collapses -> months of income erased in days. Being right on fundamentals does not protect.

loops.label: Squeeze Reflexivity
loops.constraint: OI vs deliverable float is the critical ratio; crowd positioning creates scarcity it seeks to exploit
loops.breaks_when: Short covering completes, new supply enters deliverable set, or policy intervention. Non-CTD basis narrowing toward zero = effective supply increase (self-correcting).

loops.label: Shadow Money Procyclical Loop
loops.constraint: 10-14 large dealers control global collateral rehypothecation; velocity is invisible variable
loops.breaks_when: Haircut spike -> velocity contraction -> effective liquidity collapses 20%+ -> credit freeze. Three multiplicative factors reverse simultaneously.

loops.label: Synthetic Yield Enhancement Persistence
loops.constraint: Structural mortgage hedging selling keeps 10Y futures chronically cheap; vol arbitrageurs are the equilibrating force
loops.breaks_when: Vol arb slowly closes gap, mortgage hedging flow subsides, or basis trade crowding reverses structural cheapness

loops.label: LCR Boosting Cycle
loops.constraint: Only FHLBs provide net-positive LCR boost. Category IV banks show up at month-end to bid FF from FHLBs. Unwind next day.
loops.breaks_when: Reporting date passes → borrowing unwinds → EFFR normalizes within 1-2 business days. Predictable and tradeable.

loops.label: QT Buffer Depletion Cascade
loops.constraint: RRP absorbs QT painlessly while positive (free phase). Treasury issuance mix determines drain path.
loops.breaks_when: Fed slows/stops QT, restarts QE, or conducts sufficient repo operations to replenish reserves. Sept 2019 intervention template.

loops.label: Shadow Money Boom-Bust Cycle
loops.constraint: Three multiplicative factors (value, haircut, velocity) compound: small moves create massive swings. 2008: 48% destruction.
loops.breaks_when: Public substitution (QE, T-bills, Fed facilities) offsets private contraction. If insufficient → deflationary spiral.

loops.label: ETF-Repo Fragility Link
loops.constraint: AP arbitrage funded by same repo/CP infrastructure as everything else. Funded by triparty repos and CP to MMFs.
loops.breaks_when: Repo market normalizes → AP funding restored → ETF discounts close. During stress: self-reinforcing discount spiral.

loops.label: Vol Arbitrage Equilibrium
loops.constraint: Arbs buy cheap basis / sell rich options (or reverse). Pulls OAB toward zero. Structural mortgage selling maintains persistent cheapness.
loops.breaks_when: Structural flows overwhelm arb capacity for years (golden age 1985-89). Or regime shift (death of gamma) kills optionality entirely.

loops.label: OTR Basis Richening Cycle
loops.constraint: Post-auction: short hedges → specialness → basis widens → more shorts. ~5bp richening over 1.5 months. Pre-next-auction: supply anticipation reverses.
loops.breaks_when: Next auction creates new OTR. Cycle resets. ~3 month period. Quarter-end intensifies specialness; post-quarter-end releases.

loops.label: Deliverable Float Erosion
loops.constraint: Float erodes via stripping, CB holdings, buy-and-hold. No natural replenishment unless Treasury issues at right point on curve.
loops.breaks_when: Yield regime shift creates new CTD with fresh supply. Or Treasury policy change (buybacks, maturity changes, factor coupon redesign) alters deliverable set.

loops.label: FX Bandwagon Reflexivity
loops.constraint: Medium-term attractor must be bullish/bearish (not neutral). Bandwagon power proportional to capital flow dominance ratio.
loops.breaks_when: Confidence erodes (exogenous shock, profit-taking overwhelms), mental model shifts from pro- to anti-currency, or policy intervention (capital controls, CB action) disrupts flow.

loops.label: Depreciation-Inflation Death Spiral
loops.constraint: Requires inelastic trade structure (developing nation), no domestic import substitutes, and foreign-denominated debt. Spiral velocity proportional to import dependence.
loops.breaks_when: Capital controls imposed, external lending facility activated (IMF), structural reform reduces import dependence, or currency falls far enough to attract distressed-debt capital.

loops.label: Open-Economy Minsky Fragility Buildup
loops.constraint: Financial liberalization accelerates. Boom -> optimism -> debt growth (short-term + foreign-denominated) -> bandwagon supports currency -> more borrowing -> tension accumulates at all three points simultaneously.
loops.breaks_when: Any single tension point snaps: (1) currency-forecast divergence forces devaluation, (2) financial returns collapse vs real economy, or (3) debt/income ratios breach sustainability. Mexico 1994, Thailand 1997 are completed experiments.

chains.label: The Script (Inflation-Killing Sequence)
chains.description: Coupon-heavy issuance -> term premium expansion -> long yields rise -> P/E compression -> equity decline -> wealth effect weakens -> demand falls -> inflation dies. Policymakers keep interrupting at step 2-3.

chains.label: Credit Pipeline to Inflation
chains.description: Credit impulse (4-6Q lag) -> profits -> demand -> prices (6-8Q) -> wages (1Q). Full pipeline is 2-3 years. Wages follow, never lead.

chains.label: Credit to Housing to CPI
chains.description: Credit growth -> house prices (1-2Y) -> shelter CPI (16mo) -> Core CPI (shelter = 43% weight). Dominant inflation transmission channel.

chains.label: Fiscal Deficit to Private Savings
chains.description: Deficit spending creates private savings (identity). Those savings go into bills (stimulative) or coupons (restrictive). Composition determines financial conditions.

chains.label: Debt Ceiling to RRP Drain
chains.description: Ceiling binds -> EM depletion -> bill suppressed -> TGA drains. Resolution -> aggressive bill issuance -> massive supply -> RRP drains as MMFs buy bills.

chains.label: QT Transmission Path
chains.description: Fed runoff reduces reserves. If bills: RRP drains (benign). If coupons: reserves drain (bites). Distribution matters: pockets of scarcity trigger stress.

chains.label: Commodity-Oil-Wages Chain
chains.description: Credit -> equities -> dollar weakens -> oil rises -> CPI -> wages. Wages are anchored to oil prices, not expectations. Full mediated chain.

chains.label: GEX Collapse to Crash
chains.description: GEX declines to zero -> stabilization fails -> IV spikes -> VEX turns negative -> dealer selling -> crash. Requires customer-sold puts going ITM.

chains.label: Ouroboros Reversal (Vol Death Spiral)
chains.description: Vol shock -> VAR models force deleverage -> selling -> more vol -> more deleverage -> death spiral. 1987 template. Only ends with policy intervention or exhaustion.

chains.label: Bessant-Fed Monetization Chain
chains.description: Bill-heavy issuance -> bill market approaches capacity -> Fed RMPs absorb excess -> balance sheet grows. Not QE, but expanding SOMA. Implicit monetization.

chains.label: Financial Crisis Sequence
chains.description: Unexpected losses on safe secured lending -> 50-100x provisioning spike -> credit supply freeze -> all sectors synchronized contraction -> deep recession. Requires bank recapitalization.

chains.label: Panic vs Crisis Decision Tree
chains.description: Equity drawdown -> Is credit supply freezing? NO = panic (buy dip). YES = crisis (defend). Most drawdowns are panics. The single most important real-time assessment.

chains.label: Fiscal Deficit to Equity Prices (Full Path)
chains.description: Deficit -> credit impulse -> demand -> profits -> multiples -> equity prices. Multiple paths: direct demand, wealth effect, collateral channel.

chains.label: Cross-Border Capital Composition Shift
chains.description: Post-GFC: bank paper -> equities. Structural bid for S&P. Less UST buying from foreign CBs -> term premium pressure.

chains.label: Claim Dilution to Inflation
chains.description: New debt exceeds productive capacity -> excess claims -> profits rise first -> GDP -> prices -> wages. Unifies credit-to-inflation in one causal chain.

chains.label: AI Wealth Paradox Chain
chains.description: AI valuations -> equity gains -> wealth effect -> consumption -> sticky inflation (short run). But AI productivity -> labor displacement -> disinflation (long run). Opposing forces.

chains.label: Narrative Exhaustion to Reversal
chains.description: Narrative persists -> positioning aligns -> confirming data arrives -> price doesnt respond -> signal -> positioned longs liquidate -> trend reversal.

chains.label: Valuation Decision Tree
chains.description: Tell the story -> convert to numbers -> compute break-even revenue -> apply 3P test -> check pricing vs valuation -> act. Complete investment process.

chains.label: Merton Capital Structure to Credit Spreads
chains.description: Equity = call on assets; debt = short put. Equity vol determines fair credit spread. Vol and credit must reconcile.

chains.label: Dampener Exhaustion Cascade
chains.description: Stabilizer absorbs stress (partial claims, emergency tools). Finite capacity. When exhausted -> hidden distress becomes visible -> potential crisis trigger.

chains.label: Bond Lifecycle to RV Opportunity
chains.description: New issue (OTR, special, rich, tight bid-ask) -> ages off-the-run -> specialness fades -> trades cheap to fitted curve -> RV opportunity when Z-score extreme AND carry positive AND catalyst for convergence identified.

chains.label: Basis Trade Full P/L Decomposition
chains.description: P/L = carry earned (lockable via term repo) + BNOC change (the vol bet) + delivery option exercise value. Long basis = short delivery option vol. Implied repo connects carry and BNOC. OAB cross-checks against listed options.

chains.label: Conditional Trade Construction
chains.description: Identify directional + curve/spread view -> check spread-vs-level R-squared -> construct conditional via swaptions -> DV01-weight for premium neutrality -> if worse than forwards, do the outright trade. Regime-dependent: conditionality adds nothing when beta is zero.

chains.label: Hedge Instrument Decision Tree
chains.description: Three criteria in priority: (1) tracking error (dominates in stress, R-squared=0.18 crisis), (2) relative value (widening spreads -> swaps better hedge; narrowing -> Treasuries), (3) carry (optimize across nearby tenors when tracking error similar).

chains.label: Shadow Money Contraction Cascade
chains.description: Haircuts spike -> collateral velocity contracts -> effective shadow money shrinks 20%+ -> funding stress cascades -> potential credit supply freeze. Three multiplicative factors (value, haircut, velocity) reverse simultaneously.

chains.label: FHLB Arbitrage Full Chain
chains.description: MMFs buy FHLB discount notes → FHLBs get cash, lend FF to FBOs → FBOs park at Fed (IORB) or deploy to repos/FX swaps. Each link earns 2-10bp. The chain IS what keeps EFFR anchored below IORB. FHLBs cant earn IORB (by law) → must lend → this is why FF market exists.

chains.label: QT Transmission (Full Path)
chains.description: SOMA matures → Treasury reissues → who buys determines what drains. Bills: MMFs buy from RRP (painless). Coupons: banks buy from reserves (contractionary). RRP→0 is the nonlinear cliff. Post-cliff: reserve drain → repo spikes → Fed must intervene.

chains.label: Shadow Money Collapse Sequence
chains.description: Haircuts spike 10x → velocity contracts 20% → shadow money destroyed 48% ($9.6T→$5.0T in 2008). If public substitution (QE + T-bills + facilities) < private contraction → deflationary spiral. The public sector does King Collaterals work.

chains.label: ETF Discount Cascade
chains.description: Repo stress → APs cant fund arbitrage → ETF discounts blow out (LQD/HYG 3-5% March 2020) → price discovery fails → fire sales of underlying → deeper repo stress. AP bridge = derivative of repo/CP health.

chains.label: Balance Sheet Exhaustion to Fed Intervention
chains.description: Volume growth → TLE approaches SLR minimum → dealers shed bilateral/term repos → rates spike → settlement fails → intermediation fragments → Fed activates SRF or ad hoc TOMOs to restore flow. Central clearing is the structural solution.

chains.label: Basis Option Value Full Decomposition
chains.description: Reference yield creates optionality → three drivers (parallel, systematic, nonsystematic) determine switch value → dampener partially cancels → EOM option adds BPV-based strangle → timing/wild card add residual. Total BNOC = sum of all delivery option components.

chains.label: Basis Trade Lifecycle (Entry to Exit)
chains.description: Evaluate via scenario grid (yield x curve slope matrix) → enter (short basis = short vol, capped upside) → financing at RP/RRP (hidden friction, specialness risk) → P/L = -Δ(BNOC) for CTD → exit via convergence or roll via calendar spread (mispricing = -OAB).

chains.label: Squeeze Warning to Systemic Stress
chains.description: Float erodes → OI exceeds effective supply → RP spirals toward zero → BNOC goes negative → leveraged basis positions forced to unwind → Treasury market stress → potential broader contagion. March 2020 template. No theoretical limit to squeeze magnitude.

chains.label: Death of Gamma Full Sequence
chains.description: Spread-yield correlation saturates → three drivers collapse to one → switch option killed → CTD permanently entrenched → BNOC collapses → basis trading dies → vol arb breaks. Recovery: exogenous correlation break OR contract redesign (8%→6%). 1991-93 template.

chains.label: Synthetic Bond Enhancement Engine
chains.description: OAB persistently < 0 (structural selling) → synthetic outperforms physical → vol arbs slowly close gap → but structural flows maintain cheapness. 10Y note: 19/21 quarters outperformed, avg 61bp. Synthetic has purer duration (repo DV01 hedged away by cash leg).

chains.label: Harvey Currency Crisis Full Sequence
chains.description: Bandwagon drives currency appreciation and capital inflows → Minsky fragility builds (maturity + locational mismatch) → three tension points load simultaneously → weakest tension snaps → depreciation/inflation spiral in developing nations → credit freeze if secured lending losses materialize. Mexico 1994 and Thailand 1997 are the completed experiments.

chains.label: FX Mental Model to Capital Flow Chain
chains.description: Regime determines which indicator set is active → indicators feed mental model → forecast biases (availability, representativeness, anchoring) amplify → bandwagon forms if medium-term attractor aligns → portfolio capital flows driven by forecast → exchange rate moves. The entire chain is endogenous and self-reinforcing.

chains.label: AER-BTER Persistent Divergence
chains.description: Capital flow dominance → AER decoupled from BTER → trade deficits persist indefinitely → surplus countries trapped by structural dependence on export model → surplus nations offer refinancing → further entrenches imbalance. No automatic correction mechanism exists. Exchange rates do not equilibrate trade.

chains.label: Plumbing Stress to Equity Repricing
chains.description: Repo dislocation → dealer balance sheet constrained → market-making capacity shrinks → equity spreads widen, ERP rises → equity prices fall → wealth effect weakens → consumption and nominal demand decline. Plumbing stress transmits to real economy via equity channel, not just rates.

chains.label: Credit Impulse to Vol Regime Chain
chains.description: Credit expansion → leverage growth → feeds Ouroboros vol suppression → implied vol compressed → low-persistence regime entrenched. Credit contraction → Ouroboros reversal → vol spikes → persistence regime shifts. Credit is the exogenous driver of the vol regime, not vol itself.

regimes.label: Supported Nominal Growth
regimes.description: Debt impulse positive, deficit large, growth supported. Default regime. Risk assets bid.
regimes.transition_out: Inflation tail rises (->R02), funding stress (->R05), drawdown (->R07/R08)

regimes.label: Inflation-Constrained
regimes.description: Inflation tail high, CB constrained, cannot ease on equity dip. Bonds fail as hedge. Positive stock-bond correlation.
regimes.transition_out: Inflation subsides (->R01), stagflation deepens (->R03)

regimes.label: Stagflation
regimes.description: Both growth and inflation mandates flash red. Fed paralyzed. Both tails expand. Vol spikes. Curve shape uninformative.
regimes.transition_out: One mandate dominates (->R02 or ->R04)

regimes.label: Employment Mandate Dominant
regimes.description: Labor market softening, Fed easing bias, bull steepening. Front end rallies. Wealth effect sustaining but at risk.
regimes.transition_out: Inflation reasserts (->R02), recession materializes (->R08)

transitions.condition: Labor market deterioration deepens into recession
Easing restores growth without inflation resurgence

regimes.label: Plumbing Stress
regimes.description: Reserve scarcity hits pockets. Repo rate spikes. Forced selling. Not economic -- mechanical. Sept 2019 template.
regimes.transition_out: Policy response deployed (SRF, TOMOs, RMPs) -> R01

regimes.label: Debt Ceiling Binding
regimes.description: EM depletion, bill issuance suppressed, TGA draining, front-end scarcity. Thermostat disabled.
regimes.transition_out: Congress raises/suspends limit -> R07 (refill)

transitions.condition: Congress raises or suspends debt ceiling

regimes.label: TGA Refill / Post-Resolution
regimes.description: Aggressive bill issuance, RRP draining, liquidity tightening. Bills absorb all marginal funding.
regimes.transition_out: TGA reaches policy floor -> R01

transitions.condition: TGA reaches dynamic risk policy floor

regimes.label: Credit Crisis
regimes.description: Secured lending losses, provisioning spike 50-100x, credit supply freeze. Deep recession. Requires bank recapitalization.
regimes.transition_out: Bank recap + policy intervention -> R01 at higher Omega

transitions.condition: Bank recapitalization + policy intervention; restarts at higher Omega

regimes.label: Panic / Positioning Flush
regimes.description: Leverage unwind, no credit deterioration. Recoverable. Buy the dip. Impulse still supportive.
regimes.transition_out: Positioning clears -> R01

regimes.label: Low Vol / Contango
regimes.description: IV low, term structure steep, VRP positive. Theta collection favored. Short gamma wins most days.
regimes.transition_out: Catalyst spikes vol (->R11), systemic shock (->R13)

regimes.label: Elevated Vol / Backwardation
regimes.description: IV spiked, term structure inverted, skew steep. Gamma scalping favored. Convexity pays off.
regimes.transition_out: Event resolves -> R12, or persists -> R13

regimes.label: Vol Crush (Post-Event)
regimes.description: Event resolves, IV collapses, gamma expires worthless. Short gamma wins.
regimes.transition_out: Normalizes -> R10

regimes.label: Correlation Spike / Dispersion Collapse
regimes.description: Everything correlates to 1. Index vol explodes. Dispersion collapses. Index puts pay off.
regimes.transition_out: Shock absorbed -> R11 -> R10

regimes.label: Credit Expansion Phase
regimes.description: Delta-Omega positive, profits rising, assets appreciating. Credit-asset expansion loop active.
regimes.transition_out: Omega approaches saturation (->R15), impulse turns negative (->R16)

regimes.label: Saturation Approach
regimes.description: Omega near threshold. Marginal credit ineffective. Growth collapses to ROA. Yields forced down. Japan-like.
regimes.transition_out: Credit contracts (->R16), or policy maintains stasis

regimes.label: Credit Contraction / Deleveraging
regimes.description: Delta-Omega negative. Profits falling. Assets declining. Deleveraging spiral. Deflation risk.
regimes.transition_out: Prolonged -> R15 (debt-deflation), or policy restarts cycle -> R14

transitions.condition: Policy restarts cycle at higher Omega

regimes.label: Sell-US-Assets
regimes.description: Positive equity-Treasury correlation. Dollar weakening simultaneously. Term premium spiking. De-dollarization narrative.
regimes.transition_out: Treasuries re-establish safe haven via strong auctions -> R18

regimes.label: Flight-to-Quality Intact
regimes.description: Negative equity-Treasury correlation restored. Safe haven bid. Bull flattening. Duration hedges work.
regimes.transition_out: Inflation reasserts -> R02, growth resumes -> R01

regimes.label: Fair Value Equilibrium
regimes.description: Implied ERP near historical average (~4.25%). Stocks fairly valued. Competition erodes excess returns normally.
regimes.transition_out: ERP compresses -> R20, disruption -> R22

regimes.label: Rich Pricing / Pre-Bubble
regimes.description: ERP below average (3-4%). Overvalued but not bubble. PE elevated. Narratives gaining strength.
regimes.transition_out: ERP collapses further -> R21, correction -> R19

regimes.label: Bubble (Big Market Delusion Active)
regimes.description: ERP < 2.5%. Sum of break-even revenues > TAM. Individually plausible, collectively fairy tale. 1999 template.
regimes.transition_out: Earnings collapse / narrative collapses -> R19 or R08

regimes.label: Disruption Regime
regimes.description: New technology resets competitive advantages. Life cycles compressed. Competition turbo-charged. Winners and losers sorting.
regimes.transition_out: Disruption matures, new moats form -> R19

regimes.label: Basis Active / Multi-CTD
regimes.description: Yields near CF reference; multiple bonds contending for CTD; BNOC elevated; switch option valuable; basis trading ecosystem active. Vol arb between basis and listed options possible. OAB signals exploitable.
regimes.transition_out: Yields move far from reference (->R24), spread-yield correlation saturates (->death of gamma)

transitions.condition: Yields move far from CF reference yield

regimes.label: Basis Dead / Single-CTD Entrenchment
regimes.description: One bond firmly CTD; no realistic switch; BNOC near zero; basis ≈ carry; contract degrades to single-bond forward. All delivery options deep OTM. 1991-93 dry spell template.
regimes.transition_out: Exogenous correlation break or new bond issuance changes deliverable set -> R23

transitions.condition: Exogenous correlation break or new bond issuance changes deliverable set

regimes.label: Squeeze / Delivery Failure
regimes.description: OI exceeds deliverable float; CTD on extreme special; BNOC negative; calendar spread dislocated. Market transitions from option-pricing to failure-probability regime. Negative BNOC prices delivery failure, not free money.
regimes.transition_out: Short covering + new supply -> R23, forced delivery failures -> policy intervention

transitions.condition: Short covering completes, new supply enters

regimes.label: Abundant Reserves / Full Buffer
regimes.description: RRP high ($1T+), reserves super-abundant, SOFR/EFFR in narrow 2-5bp band below IORB. Matched book spreads tight, quarter-end distortions mild. System is full. QT pain absorbed by RRP buffer.
regimes.transition_out: QT drains RRP toward zero (->R27)

regimes.label: QT Drain / Buffer Depletion
regimes.description: RRP declining under QT. MMFs rotating RRP→T-bills. Rates drifting toward upper sweet spot. Reserves still adequate but declining. Free phase ending. Post-RRP depletion → reserve scarcity (->R05).
regimes.transition_out: RRP hits zero → reserve scarcity (->R05 plumbing stress), Fed eases → R26

transitions.condition: RRP hits zero, reserve scarcity emerges

regimes.label: Offshore Dollar Squeeze
regimes.description: FX swap-implied rates spike above onshore corridor. Cross-currency basis blows out. Foreign CBs may activate swap lines (OIS+50bp ceiling). Without swap lines, forced Treasury selling creates doom loop. FIMA repo prevents forced selling.
regimes.transition_out: Swap lines + confidence → R26, failure to contain → contagion to R08 credit crisis

regimes.label: Basis Golden Age / Chronic Cheapness
regimes.description: Structural selling (mortgage hedging, dealer short-basis) keeps futures persistently below fair value. OAB < 0 for extended periods. Yield enhancement works reliably. Vol arbs equilibrate slowly but cannot overcome structural flow. 1985-89 template.
regimes.transition_out: Structural flows reverse, basis trade crowding reverses cheapness, or regime shift to death of gamma

transitions.condition: Structural flows reverse or basis crowding reverses cheapness
Regime shift to death of gamma

regimes.label: Wild Card / Inverted Carry
regimes.description: Inverted curve → negative carry. High conversion factors (high-coupon era). Intraday vol sufficient for wild card exercise after 2PM fix. Delivery timing becomes strategic. Wild card value can exceed carry bleed despite negative carry. 1979-81 template.
regimes.transition_out: Curve normalizes, vol subsides, or coupon environment changes reducing CF magnitudes

transitions.condition: Curve normalizes, vol subsides, coupon environment changes

axioms.statement: Credit impulse (second derivative of debt) drives economic inflections, not credit level
Private credit has ~3x the GDP multiplier of public deficit spending
Loans create deposits, not the reverse. Banks do not lend reserves. Credit is endogenous.
Corporate profits are proportional to total debt (Kalecki: Pi ~ 3.5% x Debt), not productivity
Debt saturation (Omega_sat) is the threshold where marginal debt stops boosting growth. Japan is the completed experiment.
QE is not money printing (asset swap). Fiscal deficits are the true money creation channel.
Wages follow prices adaptively with 1-quarter lag; expectations play a decorative, not causal, role
The bond market is everything: all asset classes price off the risk-free long rate
Term premium (not fed funds rate) determines the actual stance of financial conditions
Treasury issuance composition (bills vs coupons) is the most impactful fiscal policy decision when debt/GDP is high
Policymakers follow a fear policy: they will intervene to prevent a disorderly bond market
Protecting bonds (future money) devalues currency (spot money). Cannot save both.
The post-pandemic economy is income-driven, not credit-driven. Short rates are ineffective.
The distribution of rate outcomes matters more than the point forecast
Committee divergence structurally brakes the speed of policy repricing independent of data
Options ARE the order book: dealer delta-hedging obligations dominate index liquidity
GEX stabilizes, VEX destabilizes: crashes are driven by negative VEX, not GEX alone
Volatility is the only asset class: all strategies decompose into short vol or long vol
You cannot destroy risk; you can only transmute it in form
Stock-bond anti-correlation is a regime, not a law of physics. It has failed repeatedly over 120 years.
The modern institutional portfolio is ~98% short vol and does not know it
Price action is the primary information source; narrative is secondary and often wrong
Positioning extremes create fragile equilibria that resolve violently in max-pain direction
The economy is structurally k-shaped; aggregate data hides the distribution that drives prices
The US dollar is the master cross-asset regime variable; dollar behavior encodes the global state
Commodities drive the dollar, not the reverse (consensus has causality backward)
CA deficit = capital inflow (identity). Surplus countries must export capital.
Financial crises require unexpected losses on SAFE secured lending, not junk bonds
Most drawdowns are panics (recoverable), not crises. The distinction is THE most important real-time assessment.
Stability breeds instability: calm periods build leverage that makes the eventual correction violent
The TGA risk policy floor is dynamic (1-week forward outflows), not the static 750B number
Bills are the shock absorber: coupons are slow policy, bills are fast tactical
SLR is the binding constraint on dealer intermediation capacity
Bond price-yield relationship is inverse and convex: gains exceed losses for equal yield changes
Carry is known, volatility is uncertain. Carry-to-vol ratio is the proper risk-adjusted measure.
Mortgage negative convexity is the dominant feedback loop in rates: extension forces selling that amplifies moves
The value of any asset is the PV of expected cash flows. Earnings are not cash flows.
Competitive advantages are the only source of excess returns. Without moats, growth destroys value.
Every valuation is a bridge between a story and a number. Stories without numbers are fairy tales.
AI will reduce margins, not increase them, across the aggregate. If everybody has it, nobody has it.
Every capex boom ends in a glut. AI follows the same arc.
Bond yields track the credit pipeline, not an exogenous r*. No bond vigilantes.
Auctions bring out buyers. Supply concerns cap yields but do not break them.
TIPS are a broken barometer: dominated by liquidity premiums, not inflation expectations
The gamma-theta contest is the fundamental options P&L mechanic. Everything is always about vol.
Basis = Carry + BNOC, always, by construction. Exact, exhaustive decomposition. No third channel.
Buying the basis is buying volatility; selling the basis is selling volatility. The basis market is a synthetic options market.
Carry-to-volatility ratio is THE comparison metric across instruments, tenors, and trade structures -- not carry alone
50:50 butterflies embed hidden factor exposure (level + slope). Regression-derived weights isolate pure relative value.
Hedging converts more volatile risk to less volatile risk; it is risk conversion, not risk elimination. Worth doing only if outright vol >> resulting basis vol.
The same swap spread responds to completely different dominant factors in different regimes. Driver rotation, not addition.
Shadow money = outstanding x price x (1-haircut) x velocity. Three multiplicative factors make it the invisible monetary variable.
Haircuts lead the credit cycle; more sensitive leading indicator than interest rates. The answer lies in the shadows.
Effective money = M2 + shadow money (outstanding x price x (1-haircut) x velocity). Traditional aggregates are incomplete and misleading.
When private shadow money contracts, public shadow money must expand to prevent deflation. The public sector does King Collaterals work.
The FBO park-vs-deploy decision is the invisible hand that determines exactly where EFFR settles within the corridor on any given day.
The bill-repo spread is the access premium: bills yield below repo because captive demand from investors who cannot do repo by regulation.
RRP absorbs QT painlessly while positive. Once RRP hits zero, QT drains reserves directly. The transition is a nonlinear cliff, not gradual.
Nettable vs unnettable can be a 100x cost difference in balance sheet consumption. This single distinction dominates all other repo pricing considerations.
BNY Mellon is the single point of failure for $4.5T daily triparty repo. Its intraday credit bridges the 3:30 PM pivot. No backup exists.
Stigma makes the ceiling soft: SRF/DW exist but dealers prefer paying above-ceiling rates rather than signaling distress. Asymmetric corridor.
Conversion factors are the source of all optionality. Without the imperfect equalization at a single yield, there would be no CTD, no switching, no options, no BNOC.
The futures price traces the smooth curve below the minimum of converted prices. This geometric fact is the root of negative convexity, the option analogy, and the entire basis ecosystem.
Selling the basis is selling volatility; capped upside earned slowly via theta, uncapped downside realized suddenly via CTD switch. Every basis trade IS a volatility position.
Short CTD basis P/L = negative of the change in BNOC. Exact identity. Carry is deterministic and nets out. The entire stochastic P/L is BNOC change.
The 10-25bp RP/reverse RP spread is the hidden tax on every basis trade. Specialness can amplify to hundreds of bp. Real carry differs from screen carry.
Calendar spread mispricing = OAB with opposite sign. Buying a cheap spread, buying cheap futures, and buying a cheap basis are the same trade expressed three ways.
The deliverable float is a melting ice cube. Stripping, CB purchases, and buy-and-hold accumulation steadily reduce effective supply, making squeezes inevitable.
Spot yields and repo rates are independent forces on the forward price. R-squared ≈ 0.02 at weekly frequency. Spot DV01 ≈ 50x repo DV01. Complete hedge requires offsetting both.
Portfolio capital flows dominate currency markets over ALL time horizons, not just the short run. There is no mechanism forcing exchange rates toward balanced-trade levels.
Agents expectations are a real, causal element in FX determination, not white noise around a fundamental trend. The forecast and the object thereof are not independent.
Forecast-construction biases (availability, representativeness, anchoring) are incorporated into prices and shift economic activity onto new paths. They are not errors to be averaged away.
Currency crises emerge from three simultaneous tension points (currency-forecast divergence, financial-returns divergence, financial fragility). Any one can snap and trigger collapse.
Uncovered interest rate parity does not hold because expectations are shaped by social and psychological forces, not mechanical arbitrage. Covered parity holds because it is a definition.

forks.label: Income-Driven vs Credit-Driven Transmission

views.position: Short rates are ineffective in an income-driven economy. Fixed-rate mortgages insulate. Need long yields / asset prices to slow demand.
Credit impulse IS the engine. Private credit has 3x multiplier. Bank lending restart is the most powerful inflection point.

forks.resolution: Both can be simultaneously true: short rates work through credit channel (which is currently dormant) while the economy runs on income/wealth. The fork is about which channel dominates NOW, not which exists.

forks.label: Bond Yields: Supply-Driven vs Credit-Pipeline-Driven

views.position: Yields are primarily driven by supply/demand for duration: coupon issuance, buyer base, term premium.
Yields mechanically track the credit pipeline (credit -> GDP -> profits -> yields) with >99.999% Bayesian confidence. No bond vigilantes.

forks.resolution: These operate on different timescales. Credit pipeline drives the secular trend (years). Supply/demand for duration drives the cyclical deviation around that trend (quarters). Both are real forces.

forks.label: Volatility: Regime Variable vs Mechanical Quantity

views.position: Vol is a regime variable: the Ouroboros makes vol both input and output. Suppressed vol IS the risk. Long vol is the Dennis Rodman of portfolios.
Vol is a mechanical quantity: gamma-theta contest, hedging frequency determines measurement. Implied/realized spread is the fundamental P&L mechanic.

forks.resolution: Different levels of abstraction. The mechanical view is correct for individual trade P&L. The regime view is correct for portfolio construction and systemic risk assessment. Both are true simultaneously.

forks.label: What Drives the Dollar

views.position: Commodities drive the dollar: commodity up -> EM earnings -> capital to EM -> USD down. Causality runs opposite to consensus.
Interest rate differentials lead FX; the dollar encodes the state of the global system.

forks.resolution: Multiple channels operate simultaneously. Rate differentials dominate at cyclical frequency. Commodity/capital flow channel dominates at secular (10yr) frequency. GDP-FX parity provides the long-run anchor.

forks.label: Inflation Causation: Credit vs Expectations vs Cost-Push

views.position: Inflation is a lagged accounting cascade from credit. Expectations play a decorative role. Wages follow prices, never lead.
Inflation expectations anchoring is critical. If super-core accelerates and spills into wages, wage-price spiral risk is real.
Tariff inflation is a one-time price adjustment, not demand-driven. The Fed can look through it as long as services cooperate.

forks.resolution: Credit is the dominant secular driver. Expectations matter at the margin for credibility. Tariffs are a one-time level shift. All three views are correct in different contexts -- the question is which channel dominates in the current regime.

forks.label: Risk Premiums: Certainty Premium vs Competition Erosion

views.position: Government backstops raise sustainable P/E. Low rates are a symptom, not a cause. Certainty premiums justify higher multiples.
Competition relentlessly erodes excess returns. 70%+ of firms earn below cost of capital. Growth without moats destroys value.

forks.resolution: Both are true. Certainty premiums raise the AGGREGATE market multiple. But within the market, only moated companies earn excess returns. The aggregate masks the distribution.

forks.label: Market Efficiency: Self-Correcting vs Flow-Driven

views.position: Markets are noisy self-correcting mechanisms. Implied ERP is the best gauge. Timing is a losing strategy.
Markets are flow-driven. Dealer hedging, passive investing, and vol-targeting make markets entirely mechanical. Price action is the primary signal.

forks.resolution: Different timescales. Flow-driven at intraday/weekly. Self-correcting at quarterly/annual. Valuation works over years; flows dominate over days.

forks.label: Plumbing: Macro Overlay vs Mechanical Layer

views.position: Reserves, RRP, QT -- these are macro variables. QT drains liquidity. Bills vs coupons determines whether QT bites.
The plumbing is mechanical. Rate corridor, settlement timing, dealer capacity, nettability. Understand the pipes, not the narrative.

forks.resolution: Both levels are needed. The macro view determines WHAT the plumbing does to the economy. The mechanical view determines HOW it does it and WHERE it breaks.

forks.label: AI: Disruption Bubble vs Structural Transformation

views.position: Big market delusion. Sum of break-even revenues > TAM. Individually plausible, collectively fairy tale. Correction inevitable.
AI is structurally disinflationary and transformative. Productivity gains are real. But wealth effect is reflationary short-term.

forks.resolution: Architecture companies (chips, data centers) are protected -- revenue is real because capex is being spent. Product/service companies (LLMs) are where delusion forms. The correction is healthy: three steps forward, two steps back.

forks.label: Gold: Catastrophe Insurance vs Broken Signal

views.position: Gold is a collectible, not an asset. Cannot be valued. Weak hedge against normal events. Only matters for hyperinflation/catastrophe.
TIPS are the broken signal. Gold and equity multiples provide cleaner real-rate and inflation signals than TIPS.

forks.resolution: Gold serves different functions depending on allocation size. As core investment: terrible. As insurance (15-20%): effective against catastrophe. As signal: rising gold may indicate structural mistrust of financial system.

forks.label: Basis Trading: Vol Arb vs Carry Machine

views.position: Basis is a synthetic options market. OAB measures mispricing between basis-implied vol and listed options. Trade the discrepancy. Basis and exchange options should agree on vol.
Basis is a leveraged carry machine. Long basis (buy cash, sell futures) earns carry plus BNOC convergence. Leverage 50-100x via repo. The dominant source of cash Treasury demand.

forks.resolution: Both are simultaneously true for different participants. Vol arb traders monitor OAB and cross-check basis vol with listed options. Carry traders run leveraged basis for income. The carry crowd provides the persistent cheapness that vol arb traders exploit.

forks.label: Vol Surface: Structural Flows vs Rate Expectations

views.position: Vol surface shape reflects rate expectations. Short-expiry high because near-term uncertainty; long-expiry low because mean reversion. Skew reflects distribution beliefs.
Vol surface shape is determined by structural flows. GSE demand supports intermediate vol; callable issuance depresses long-end; range accruals compress cap vol. Expectations are secondary.

forks.resolution: Both forces operate. Expectations determine the broad level and slope. Structural flows create persistent kinks and richness/cheapness at specific expiry-tail points. Trading the surface means knowing which force dominates at each point.

forks.label: QT Impact: Painless Drain vs Reserve Scarcity

views.position: QT is painless: RRP buffer absorbs the drain, reserves unchanged, market functioning smooth. Bill issuance draws MMFs from RRP. The free phase.
QT is contractionary: RRP depleted, reserves drain directly, repo rates spike, settlement fails, Fed must intervene. The painful phase.

forks.resolution: Both are true at different phases. Free phase (RRP>0): painless. Painful phase (RRP near 0): contractionary. The transition is a cliff, not gradual. Treasury issuance mix (bills vs coupons) determines which phase the system is in.

forks.label: Shadow Money: Invisible Variable vs Noise

views.position: Shadow money is THE missing variable. Traditional M2 understates true money by trillions. Haircuts, not rates, are the binding constraint on credit. 2008 = 48% shadow money destruction.
Credit impulse and bank lending are the primary monetary variables. Shadow money adds complexity but credit impulse drives the cycle. Central bank reserves and lending standards matter more.

forks.resolution: Different layers of the same system. Credit impulse drives the cycle at macro frequency. Shadow money determines severity of turns. At inflection points (2008, 2020), shadow money dynamics dominate. During calm, credit impulse dominates.

forks.label: Basis Trade: Short Vol Machine vs Structural Arb

views.position: Basis selling is a short-vol machine. Win 23/25 quarters but catastrophic losses in the other 2. Same psychology as all short-vol strategies. Draws crowding via deceptive high win rate.
Basis is a structural arb. OAB = fair value gap. Vol arbs keep it near zero. When structural flows create persistent mispricing, exploitation is fair-value capture, not vol-selling.

forks.resolution: Both simultaneously true for different participants. Carry-oriented sellers are short vol. Vol arb traders exploit OAB discrepancies against listed options. The carry crowd provides persistent cheapness the vol arb traders exploit. The two populations need each other.

forks.label: FX Determination: Capital Flows vs Trade Flows vs Credit Pipeline

views.position: Capital flows dominate. There is no automatic tendency for exchange rates to restore balanced trade. AER and BTER can diverge indefinitely. Portfolio investors expectations are the causal force.
Trade flows determine long-run exchange rates. Capital flows are white noise or accommodate trade. PPP holds over very long horizons. Arbitrage restores equilibrium.
Credit impulse drives both via GDP. GDP-FX parity is the long-run anchor. Capital and trade are both downstream of credit creation. Currencies are shares of global production.

forks.resolution: Capital flows dominate cyclical/medium-term FX movements. Credit pipeline provides the secular anchor via GDP-FX parity. Trade flows matter only when agents choose to focus on them (indicator rotation). Three timescales, three dominant forces: capital flows (months), credit pipeline (years), trade structure (decades).
